              "before": "# User Flows\n\n> **Purpose**: Complete user journey maps for v2.0  \n> **Format**: Mermaid diagrams + step-by-step descriptions  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Core User Flows\n\n### Flow 1: New User First-Time Experience\n\n**Goal**: User successfully processes their first video\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[Land on /chat] --> B{Authenticated?}\n    B -->|No| C[Redirect to /login]\n    B -->|Yes| D[Show Empty State]\n    \n    D --> E[Display Guidance Text]\n    E --> F[Show Community Demo Cards]\n    \n    F --> G{User Action}\n    G -->|Paste URL| H[User Types YouTube URL]\n    G -->|Click Demo| I[Open Demo in Panel]\n    \n    H --> J[AI Detects URL]\n    J --> K[Show Processing Card]\n    K --> L[Backend Creates Task]\n    L --> M[Realtime Updates Card]\n    M --> N[Card Shows Completed]\n    N --> O[Panel Auto-Opens]\n    O --> P[User Sees Video + Summary]\n    \n    P --> Q[User Asks Follow-up Question]\n    Q --> R[AI Answers Using Context]\n    R --> S[Success: Engaged User]\n```\n\n**Step-by-Step Breakdown**:\n\n1. **Landing** (0s)\n   - URL: `/chat`\n   - UI: Empty chat with guidance text\n   - Action: Read \"Paste a YouTube URL to start...\"\n\n2. **Discover** (5-10s)\n   - UI: Scroll down, see Community Demo Cards\n   - Option A: Click demo → Skip to step 7\n   - Option B: Paste own URL → Continue to step 3\n\n3. **Submission** (10-15s)\n   - User pastes: `https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123`\n   - UI: Input shows URL with preview thumbnail\n   - Action: Press Enter or click Send\n\n4. **Processing Starts** (15-16s)\n   - AI sends message: \"I'll analyze this video for you\"\n   - UI: Shows processing card with spinner\n   - Backend: Creates task, starts workflow\n\n5. **Progress Updates** (16-45s)\n   - Card updates: \"Downloading... (Step 1/4)\"\n   - Card updates: \"Transcribing... (Step 2/4)\"\n   - Card updates: \"Summarizing... (Step 3/4)\"\n   - Realtime: Supabase pushes status changes\n\n6. **Completion** (45-50s)\n   - Card shows: \"✓ Ready\"\n   - Button appears: \"View Summary\"\n   - Panel auto-opens on right side\n\n7. **Exploration** (50s+)\n   - User sees video player + summary tabs\n   - User clicks keypoint → Video seeks to timestamp\n   - User asks: \"What was the main conclusion?\"\n   - AI responds using summary context\n\n**Success Criteria**:\n- ✅ User completes flow in <2 minutes\n- ✅ User sends ≥1 follow-up question\n- ✅ User doesn't encounter errors\n\n---\n\n### Flow 2: Returning User - Access Past Videos\n\n**Goal**: User finds and reopens a previously processed video\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[Open /chat] --> B[See Recent Threads]\n    \n    B --> C{Find Video?}\n    C -->|In Recent| D[Click Thread in List]\n    C -->|Not Recent| E[Click Library Button]\n    \n    E --> F[Library Sidebar Opens]\n    F --> G{Search or Browse?}\n    G -->|Search| H[Type in Search Box]\n    G -->|Browse| I[Scroll Through List]\n    \n    H --> J[Results Filter Live]\n    J --> K[Click Result]\n    \n    I --> K\n    D --> K\n    \n    K --> L[Load Thread Messages]\n    L --> M[Open Video Panel]\n    M --> N[Resume Conversation]\n```\n\n**Step-by-Step**:\n\n1. **Open Chat** (0s)\n   - URL: `/chat`\n   - UI: Shows last active thread (if any)\n\n2. **Trigger Library** (2s)\n   - Click \"☰ Library\" button (or press `Cmd+K`)\n   - Sidebar slides in from left\n\n3. **Find Video** (5-15s)\n   - **Option A - Browse**: Scroll through chronological list\n   - **Option B - Search**: Type title/keyword\n   - List shows: Title + Timestamp + Platform icon\n\n4. **Open** (15s)\n   - Click desired video entry\n   - Library sidebar closes\n   - Thread loads (if exists) OR creates new thread\n   - Panel opens with video details\n\n5. **Continue** (20s+)\n   - User asks new questions about the video\n   - AI has full context from original analysis\n\n---\n\n### Flow 3: Multi-URL Handling\n\n**Goal**: User pastes multiple YouTube URLs, system handles gracefully\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Types Message with 2+ URLs] --> B[Frontend Detects Multiple URLs]\n    B --> C[Show Confirmation Dialog]\n    \n    C --> D{User Choice}\n    D -->|Process Separately| E[Create 2 Tasks + 2 Threads]\n    D -->|Compare Together| F[Create 2 Tasks + 1 Thread]\n    D -->|Cancel| G[Return to Input]\n    \n    E --> H1[Thread 1 Active]\n    E --> H2[Thread 2 Created]\n    H1 --> I[User Can Switch Threads]\n    \n    F --> J[Both Videos in Same Chat]\n    J --> K[AI Can Compare Content]\n```\n\n**Dialog UI**:\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Detected 2 video links:             │\n│ • How AI Works (5:32)               │\n│ • Machine Learning Intro (12:15)    │\n│                                     │\n│ How should I process them?          │\n│ ⚪ Separately (2 conversations)      │\n│ ⚪ Together (compare in one chat)    │\n│                                     │\n│ [Cancel] [Confirm]                  │\n└─────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n### Flow 4: Error Recovery\n\n**Goal**: User encounters error, system guides them to recovery\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Submits URL] --> B[Processing Starts]\n    B --> C{Error Occurs}\n    \n    C -->|Invalid URL| D[Show: URL Not Supported]\n    C -->|Network Fail| E[Show: Connection Lost]\n    C -->|Quota Exceeded| F[Show: Upgrade Prompt]\n    \n    D --> G[Suggest Valid Format]\n    E --> H[Retry Button]\n    F --> I[Link to Pricing]\n    \n    G --> J[User Corrects URL]\n    H --> K[Auto-Retry Backend]\n    I --> L[User Upgrades Plan]\n    \n    J --> M[Success]\n    K --> M\n    L --> M\n```\n\n**Error Messages** (User-Friendly):\n\n| Error Type | Message | Recovery Action |\n|------------|---------|-----------------|\n| Invalid URL | \"This doesn't look like a YouTube URL. Try: https://youtube.com/watch?v=...\" | Show format example |\n| Private Video | \"This video is private. Please check the link and try again.\" | Suggest checking permissions |\n| Network Error | \"Connection lost. Retrying in 3s...\" | Auto-retry 3 times |\n| Quota Exceeded | \"You've used all 5 free videos this month. Upgrade to Pro?\" | Show upgrade CTA |\n| Processing Failed | \"Something went wrong. Our team has been notified. Try again in a few minutes.\" | Manual retry button |\n\n---\n\n## 📱 Mobile-Specific Flows\n\n### Flow 5: Mobile Video Viewing\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User on Mobile] --> B[Paste URL in Chat]\n    B --> C[Processing Card Appears]\n    C --> D[Tap Video Card]\n    \n    D --> E[Sheet Slides Up from Bottom]\n    E --> F[Video Player Fills Screen]\n    F --> G{User Action}\n    \n    G -->|Swipe Down| H[Close Sheet, Back to Chat]\n    G -->|Tap Summary Tab| I[Switch to Summary View]\n    G -->|Ask Question| J[Input Appears at Bottom]\n    \n    J --> K[Question Posted to Chat]\n    K --> H[Sheet Closes, Shows Answer]\n```\n\n**Mobile Gestures**:\n- **Swipe Down**: Close video detail sheet\n- **Swipe Left/Right**: Switch between Summary/Script tabs\n- **Long Press**: Copy text from summary\n- **Double Tap**: Zoom video (if native player)\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Edge Cases & Alternate Paths\n\n### Edge Case 1: Very Long Video (>2 hours)\n\n**Flow**:\n1. User submits 3-hour lecture\n2. System detects duration\n3. Shows warning: \"This video is 3h long. Processing may take 5-10 minutes.\"\n4. User confirms or cancels\n5. If confirmed: Show extended progress bar with estimated time\n\n### Edge Case 2: Unsupported Platform\n\n**Flow**:\n1. User pastes Vimeo URL (not supported yet)\n2. System shows: \"Currently only YouTube, Bilibili, and Xiaoyuzhou are supported.\"\n3. Offers: \"Request support for Vimeo?\"\n4. If yes: Opens feedback form\n\n### Edge Case 3: Duplicate URL in Same Thread\n\n**Flow**:\n1. User pastes URL already processed in current thread\n2. System detects duplicate\n3. Shows: \"You already analyzed this video in this chat. Would you like to open it again?\"\n4. If yes: Opens panel without reprocessing\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Flow Validation Criteria\n\n**Each flow should**:\n- Have ≤5 user actions to complete\n- Provide feedback within 200ms of each action\n- Offer clear recovery path for errors\n- Work identically on desktop and mobile (except gestures)\n\n**Next**: See `ux/11_wireframes.md` for visual layouts of these flows.\n",

Which one would you  │\n│ like me to analyze?                │\n│                                    │\n│ 1️⃣ How AI Works (5:32)             │\n│    https://youtube.com/watch?v=A   │\n│                                    │\n│ 2️⃣ ML Intro (12:15)                │\n│    https://youtube.com/watch?v=B   │\n│                                    │\n│ Reply with 1 or 2, or paste a      │\n│ single URL to start fresh.         │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Alternative: Frontend Prevention**\n```\nInput Box:\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Paste https://youtube.com/1...     │\n│ https://youtube.com/2...           │\n│                                  📤│\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n                ↓\n        (User clicks Send)\n                ↓\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ ⚠️ Please remove extra URLs        │\n│ One video per conversation         │\n│                              [OK]  │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n### Flow 4: Error Recovery\n\n**Goal**: User encounters error, system guides them to recovery\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Submits URL] --> B[Processing Starts]\n    B --> C{Error Occurs}\n    \n    C -->|Invalid URL| D[Show: URL Not Supported]\n    C -->|Network Fail| E[Show: Connection Lost]\n    C -->|Quota Exceeded| F[Show: Upgrade Prompt]\n    \n    D --> G[Suggest Valid Format]\n    E --> H[Retry Button]\n    F --> I[Link to Pricing]\n    \n    G --> J[User Corrects URL]\n    H --> K[Auto-Retry Backend]\n    I --> L[User Upgrades Plan]\n    \n    J --> M[Success]\n    K --> M\n    L --> M\n```\n\n**Error Messages** (User-Friendly):\n\n| Error Type | Message | Recovery Action |\n|------------|---------|-----------------|\n| Invalid URL | \"This doesn't look like a YouTube URL. Try: https://youtube.com/watch?v=...\" | Show format example |\n| Private Video | \"This video is private. Please check the link and try again.\" | Suggest checking permissions |\n| Network Error | \"Connection lost. Retrying in 3s...\" | Auto-retry 3 times |\n| Quota Exceeded | \"You've used all 5 free videos this month. Upgrade to Pro?\" | Show upgrade CTA |\n| Processing Failed | \"Something went wrong. Our team has been notified.               "newString": "### ADR-007: Multi-URL Handling Strategy\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nUsers may paste multiple YouTube URLs in a single message. We need to define how the system handles this scenario.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Process All Simultaneously**: Create multiple tasks in parallel\n2. **Process Separately**: Create multiple threads, one per URL\n3. **Ask User to Choose One**: Only allow one URL per thread\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (One URL Per Thread)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Enforces clear context (one conversation = one video)\n- Prevents confusion in chat history\n- Aligns with 1:1 Thread-Task relationship (ADR-003)\n- Simpler implementation (no batch processing logic)\n- Users can create multiple threads if needed\n\n**User Experience**:\nWhen multiple URLs detected:\n```\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ ⚠️  Multiple URLs Detected         │\n│                                    │\n│ I can only process one video per   │\n│ conversation. Which one would you  │\n│ like me to analyze?                │\n│                                    │\n│ 1️⃣ https://youtube.com/watch?v=A  │\n│ 2️⃣ https://youtube.com/watch?v=B  │\n│                                    │\n│ Reply with 1 or 2, or paste a      │\n│ single URL to start fresh.         │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear, predictable behavior\n- ✅ No ambiguity in chat context\n- ✅ Simpler state management\n- ❌ Users must create separate threads for multiple videos (acceptable)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-008: AI Tool Calling Implementation\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to integrate video processing into chat workflow.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Vercel AI SDK Tools**: Use built-in `tools` parameter\n2. **Custom Function Calling**: Parse messages manually\n3. **Hybrid**: AI SDK for parsing, custom for execution\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Vercel AI SDK Tools)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Native support in AI SDK (`useChat` hook)\n- Handles streaming automatically\n- Standard interface (easier for future LLM swaps)\n- Well-documented patterns\n\n**Implementation**:\n```typescript\nconst { messages } = useChat({\n  api: '/api/chat',\n  tools: {\n    process_video: {\n      description: 'Process a YouTube video',\n      parameters: z.object({\n        video_url: z.string().url(),\n        language: z.string().optional(),\n      }),\n      execute: async ({ video_url, language }) => {\n        // Call backend /api/process-video\n        return { task_id, status }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n})\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Leverages existing infrastructure\n- ✅ Future-proof (tool calling is LLM standard)\n- ❌ Tied to AI SDK (but acceptable trade-off)"

                "file": "/Volumes/ssd/AI-Video-Transcriber-Frontend-Opt/docs/redesign/reference/decisions.md",
                "before": "# Architecture Decisions Record (ADR)\n\n> **Purpose**: Document key design decisions and their rationale  \n> **Format**: Each decision includes Context, Options Considered, Decision, Consequences  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## Decision Log\n\n### ADR-001: Split-Screen Layout (60/40 Fixed Ratio)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18  \n**Deciders**: Product Team\n\n**Context**:\nUser needs to simultaneously view chat conversation and video content. We evaluated different layout approaches.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Fixed 60/40**: Chat占60%，Video Panel占40%\n2. **Resizable**: 用户可拖拽调整比例\n3. **Preset Layouts**: 提供3个预设（50/50, 60/40, 70/30）\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Fixed 60/40)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Simplifies initial implementation (no drag logic needed)\n- 60/40 ratio tested well in similar tools (Perplexity, Notion AI)\n- Reduces edge cases (min/max width handling)\n- Can upgrade to resizable in v2.1 if needed\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Faster development\n- ✅ Consistent UX across users\n- ❌ Less flexibility for power users\n- ⚠️ May need adjustment based on user feedback\n\n---\n\n### ADR-002: Component Extraction Strategy\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\n`TaskDetailClient.tsx` is 1000+ lines and tightly coupled to the standalone task page. We need to reuse video display logic in the chat panel.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Duplicate Code**: Copy-paste logic into new `VideoDetailPanel`\n2. **Extract Shared Components**: Break `TaskDetailClient` into reusable pieces\n3. **Wrapper Component**: Keep `TaskDetailClient` as-is, wrap it in panel\n\n**Decision**: Option 2 (Extract Shared Components)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Adheres to DRY principle\n- Makes components testable in isolation\n- Easier to maintain (single source of truth)\n- Follows React best practices\n\n**Extraction Plan**:\n```\nTaskDetailClient (monolith)\n  ↓\n  ├── VideoPlayer (extract)\n  ├── SummarySection (extract)\n  ├── ScriptTimeline (already exists)\n  └── MindMapView (extract)\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Reusable components across app\n- ✅ Easier unit testing\n- ❌ Requires refactoring existing code\n- ⚠️ Risk of breaking current task page (mitigated by tests)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-003: Thread-Task Relationship (1:1 Model)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to decide how chat threads relate to video tasks.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **1:1**: One Thread = One Task (focused conversations)\n2. **1:N**: One Thread can contain multiple Tasks (comparison mode)\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (1:1 Model)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Simpler mental model for users\n- Easier to implement (straightforward FK relationship)\n- Aligns with \"Chat about a specific video\" UX\n- Can extend to 1:N later if comparison feature is needed\n\n**Database Design**:\n```sql\nALTER TABLE chat_threads \nADD COLUMN task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id);\n\n-- Optional: Enforce uniqueness\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_chat_threads_task_id_unique \nON chat_threads(task_id) WHERE task_id IS NOT NULL;\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear context (each conversation is about one video)\n- ✅ Simple data model\n- ❌ Users can't compare multiple videos in one chat (can add later)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-004: Library UI (Simple List)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nLibrary sidebar needs to show user's processed videos.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Simple List**: Title + timestamp\n2. **Grouped List**: Group by date (Today, Yesterday, etc.)\n3. **Rich Cards**: Thumbnail + title + summary preview\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Simple List) for MVP\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Minimalist design matches chat-first UX\n- Fast to implement\n- Low cognitive load (focuses on titles)\n- Can upgrade to grouped/rich view later\n\n**Future Enhancement Path**:\n```\nv2.0: Simple list (title + time)\n  ↓\nv2.1: Add date grouping\n  ↓\nv2.2: Add thumbnail preview\n  ↓\nv2.3: Add tag filtering\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clean, fast interface\n- ✅ Easy to scan long lists\n- ❌ Less visual (no thumbnails)\n- ⚠️ May need search for power users with 100+ tasks\n\n---\n\n### ADR-005: Mobile UX (Modal/Drawer Pattern)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nMobile screens (<768px) can't fit split-screen layout.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Stack Layout**: Chat on top, scroll to video details below\n2. **Tab Switching**: [Chat] [Video] tabs\n3. **Modal/Drawer**: Chat as main view, video details in modal\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (Modal/Drawer)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Follows mobile design standards (iOS/Android patterns)\n- Clear hierarchy (chat is primary, video is secondary)\n- No accidental scrolling issues\n- Easy to dismiss (swipe down to close)\n\n**Implementation**:\n```tsx\n// Desktop (≥768px)\n<div className=\"flex\">\n  <ChatContainer />\n  <VideoDetailPanel />\n</div>\n\n// Mobile (<768px)\n<div>\n  <ChatContainer />\n  <Sheet open={isVideoOpen}>\n    <VideoDetailPanel />\n  </Sheet>\n</div>\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Native mobile feel\n- ✅ Consistent with other mobile apps\n- ❌ Requires managing modal state\n- ⚠️ Need smooth animations for good UX\n\n---\n\n### ADR-006: Empty State Design (Guidance + Demo Cards)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nWhen user first opens chat (no messages), we need to guide them.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Blank Canvas**: Just input box, no guidance\n2. **Onboarding Tutorial**: 3-step walkthrough\n3. **Guidance + Examples**: Text prompt + demo cards\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (Guidance + Examples)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Balances simplicity and discoverability\n- Reuses existing `CommunityTemplates` component\n- Shows product value immediately (via demos)\n- Non-intrusive (no forced tutorial)\n\n**Design**:\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Paste a YouTube URL to start   │\n│  Example: https://youtu.be/...  │\n│                                 │\n│  ────── Community Examples ──── │\n│  [Demo Card 1] [Demo Card 2]    │\n│  [Demo Card 3] [Demo Card 4]    │\n└─────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear call-to-action\n- ✅ Showcases product capabilities\n- ✅ Reduces bounce rate (engagement hook)\n- ❌ Slightly more visual noise\n\n---\n\n### ADR-007: AI Tool Calling Implementation\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to integrate video processing into chat workflow.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Vercel AI SDK Tools**: Use built-in `tools` parameter\n2. **Custom Function Calling**: Parse messages manually\n3. **Hybrid**: AI SDK for parsing, custom for execution\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Vercel AI SDK Tools)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Native support in AI SDK (`useChat` hook)\n- Handles streaming automatically\n- Standard interface (easier for future LLM swaps)\n- Well-documented patterns\n\n**Implementation**:\n```typescript\nconst { messages } = useChat({\n  api: '/api/chat',\n  tools: {\n    process_video: {\n      description: 'Process a YouTube video',\n      parameters: z.object({\n        video_url: z.string().url(),\n        language: z.string().optional(),\n      }),\n      execute: async ({ video_url, language }) => {\n        // Call backend /api/process-video\n        return { task_id, status }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n})\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Leverages existing infrastructure\n- ✅ Future-proof (tool calling is LLM standard)\n- ❌ Tied to AI SDK (but acceptable trade-off)\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n**Q1**: Should we support multiple URL processing in one message?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Proposed Solution**: Show confirmation dialog asking user intent (separate threads vs. compare mode)\n\n**Q2**: How to handle Library pagination for users with 500+ tasks?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Options**: Infinite scroll vs. Load more button vs. Virtual scrolling\n\n**Q3**: Should we preserve the standalone `/tasks/[id]` page for SEO/sharing?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Impact**: Old links break vs. Maintain two views\n\n---\n\n## Future Decisions (Post-MVP)\n\n- **Keyboard Shortcuts**: Define comprehensive shortcut map (Cmd+K, Cmd+/, etc.)\n- **Drag-to-Resize**: Add resizable panel divider\n- **Offline Support**: Handle disconnected state gracefully\n- **Voice Input**: Allow voice-to-text for chat input\n\n---\n\n## Decision Review Process\n\n**When to Update This Document**:\n- Before implementing any major architectural change\n- When user feedback contradicts a decision\n- When technical constraints invalidate an option\n\n**Review Frequency**: Monthly or after each major release\n\n**Last Reviewed**: 2025-01-18\n",
                "after": "# Architecture Decisions Record (ADR)\n\n> **Purpose**: Document key design decisions and their rationale  \n> **Format**: Each decision includes Context, Options Considered, Decision, Consequences  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## Decision Log\n\n### ADR-001: Split-Screen Layout (60/40 Fixed Ratio)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18  \n**Deciders**: Product Team\n\n**Context**:\nUser needs to simultaneously view chat conversation and video content. We evaluated different layout approaches.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Fixed 60/40**: Chat占60%，Video Panel占40%\n2. **Resizable**: 用户可拖拽调整比例\n3. **Preset Layouts**: 提供3个预设（50/50, 60/40, 70/30）\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Fixed 60/40)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Simplifies initial implementation (no drag logic needed)\n- 60/40 ratio tested well in similar tools (Perplexity, Notion AI)\n- Reduces edge cases (min/max width handling)\n- Can upgrade to resizable in v2.1 if needed\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Faster development\n- ✅ Consistent UX across users\n- ❌ Less flexibility for power users\n- ⚠️ May need adjustment based on user feedback\n\n---\n\n### ADR-002: Component Extraction Strategy\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\n`TaskDetailClient.tsx` is 1000+ lines and tightly coupled to the standalone task page. We need to reuse video display logic in the chat panel.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Duplicate Code**: Copy-paste logic into new `VideoDetailPanel`\n2. **Extract Shared Components**: Break `TaskDetailClient` into reusable pieces\n3. **Wrapper Component**: Keep `TaskDetailClient` as-is, wrap it in panel\n\n**Decision**: Option 2 (Extract Shared Components)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Adheres to DRY principle\n- Makes components testable in isolation\n- Easier to maintain (single source of truth)\n- Follows React best practices\n\n**Extraction Plan**:\n```\nTaskDetailClient (monolith)\n  ↓\n  ├── VideoPlayer (extract)\n  ├── SummarySection (extract)\n  ├── ScriptTimeline (already exists)\n  └── MindMapView (extract)\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Reusable components across app\n- ✅ Easier unit testing\n- ❌ Requires refactoring existing code\n- ⚠️ Risk of breaking current task page (mitigated by tests)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-003: Thread-Task Relationship (1:1 Model)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to decide how chat threads relate to video tasks.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **1:1**: One Thread = One Task (focused conversations)\n2. **1:N**: One Thread can contain multiple Tasks (comparison mode)\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (1:1 Model)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Simpler mental model for users\n- Easier to implement (straightforward FK relationship)\n- Aligns with \"Chat about a specific video\" UX\n- Can extend to 1:N later if comparison feature is needed\n\n**Database Design**:\n```sql\nALTER TABLE chat_threads \nADD COLUMN task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id);\n\n-- Optional: Enforce uniqueness\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_chat_threads_task_id_unique \nON chat_threads(task_id) WHERE task_id IS NOT NULL;\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear context (each conversation is about one video)\n- ✅ Simple data model\n- ❌ Users can't compare multiple videos in one chat (can add later)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-004: Library UI (Simple List)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nLibrary sidebar needs to show user's processed videos.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Simple List**: Title + timestamp\n2. **Grouped List**: Group by date (Today, Yesterday, etc.)\n3. **Rich Cards**: Thumbnail + title + summary preview\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Simple List) for MVP\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Minimalist design matches chat-first UX\n- Fast to implement\n- Low cognitive load (focuses on titles)\n- Can upgrade to grouped/rich view later\n\n**Future Enhancement Path**:\n```\nv2.0: Simple list (title + time)\n  ↓\nv2.1: Add date grouping\n  ↓\nv2.2: Add thumbnail preview\n  ↓\nv2.3: Add tag filtering\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clean, fast interface\n- ✅ Easy to scan long lists\n- ❌ Less visual (no thumbnails)\n- ⚠️ May need search for power users with 100+ tasks\n\n---\n\n### ADR-005: Mobile UX (Modal/Drawer Pattern)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nMobile screens (<768px) can't fit split-screen layout.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Stack Layout**: Chat on top, scroll to video details below\n2. **Tab Switching**: [Chat] [Video] tabs\n3. **Modal/Drawer**: Chat as main view, video details in modal\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (Modal/Drawer)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Follows mobile design standards (iOS/Android patterns)\n- Clear hierarchy (chat is primary, video is secondary)\n- No accidental scrolling issues\n- Easy to dismiss (swipe down to close)\n\n**Implementation**:\n```tsx\n// Desktop (≥768px)\n<div className=\"flex\">\n  <ChatContainer />\n  <VideoDetailPanel />\n</div>\n\n// Mobile (<768px)\n<div>\n  <ChatContainer />\n  <Sheet open={isVideoOpen}>\n    <VideoDetailPanel />\n  </Sheet>\n</div>\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Native mobile feel\n- ✅ Consistent with other mobile apps\n- ❌ Requires managing modal state\n- ⚠️ Need smooth animations for good UX\n\n---\n\n### ADR-006: Empty State Design (Guidance + Demo Cards)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nWhen user first opens chat (no messages), we need to guide them.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Blank Canvas**: Just input box, no guidance\n2. **Onboarding Tutorial**: 3-step walkthrough\n3. **Guidance + Examples**: Text prompt + demo cards\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (Guidance + Examples)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Balances simplicity and discoverability\n- Reuses existing `CommunityTemplates` component\n- Shows product value immediately (via demos)\n- Non-intrusive (no forced tutorial)\n\n**Design**:\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Paste a YouTube URL to start   │\n│  Example: https://youtu.be/...  │\n│                                 │\n│  ────── Community Examples ──── │\n│  [Demo Card 1] [Demo Card 2]    │\n│  [Demo Card 3] [Demo Card 4]    │\n└─────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear call-to-action\n- ✅ Showcases product capabilities\n- ✅ Reduces bounce rate (engagement hook)\n- ❌ Slightly more visual noise\n\n---\n\n### ADR-007: Multi-URL Handling Strategy\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nUsers may paste multiple YouTube URLs in a single message. We need to define how the system handles this scenario.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Process All Simultaneously**: Create multiple tasks in parallel\n2. **Process Separately**: Create multiple threads, one per URL\n3. **Ask User to Choose One**: Only allow one URL per thread\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (One URL Per Thread)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Enforces clear context (one conversation = one video)\n- Prevents confusion in chat history\n- Aligns with 1:1 Thread-Task relationship (ADR-003)\n- Simpler implementation (no batch processing logic)\n- Users can create multiple threads if needed\n\n**User Experience**:\nWhen multiple URLs detected:\n```\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ ⚠️  Multiple URLs Detected         │\n│                                    │\n│ I can only process one video per   │\n│ conversation. Which one would you  │\n│ like me to analyze?                │\n│                                    │\n│ 1️⃣ https://youtube.com/watch?v=A  │\n│ 2️⃣ https://youtube.com/watch?v=B  │\n│                                    │\n│ Reply with 1 or 2, or paste a      │\n│ single URL to start fresh.         │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear, predictable behavior\n- ✅ No ambiguity in chat context\n- ✅ Simpler state management\n- ❌ Users must create separate threads for multiple videos (acceptable)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-008: AI Tool Calling Implementation\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to integrate video processing into chat workflow.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Vercel AI SDK Tools**: Use built-in `tools` parameter\n2. **Custom Function Calling**: Parse messages manually\n3. **Hybrid**: AI SDK for parsing, custom for execution\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Vercel AI SDK Tools)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Native support in AI SDK (`useChat` hook)\n- Handles streaming automatically\n- Standard interface (easier for future LLM swaps)\n- Well-documented patterns\n\n**Implementation**:\n```typescript\nconst { messages } = useChat({\n  api: '/api/chat',\n  tools: {\n    process_video: {\n      description: 'Process a YouTube video',\n      parameters: z.object({\n        video_url: z.string().url(),\n        language: z.string().optional(),\n      }),\n      execute: async ({ video_url, language }) => {\n        // Call backend /api/process-video\n        return { task_id, status }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n})\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Leverages existing infrastructure\n- ✅ Future-proof (tool calling is LLM standard)\n- ❌ Tied to AI SDK (but acceptable trade-off)\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n**Q1**: Should we support multiple URL processing in one message?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Proposed Solution**: Show confirmation dialog asking user intent (separate threads vs. compare mode)\n\n**Q2**: How to handle Library pagination for users with 500+ tasks?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Options**: Infinite scroll vs. Load more button vs. Virtual scrolling\n\n**Q3**: Should we preserve the standalone `/tasks/[id]` page for SEO/sharing?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Impact**: Old links break vs. Maintain two views\n\n---\n\n## Future Decisions (Post-MVP)\n\n- **Keyboard Shortcuts**: Define comprehensive shortcut map (Cmd+K, Cmd+/, etc.)\n- **Drag-to-Resize**: Add resizable panel divider\n- **Offline Support**: Handle disconnected state gracefully\n- **Voice Input**: Allow voice-to-text for chat input\n\n---\n\n## Decision Review Process\n\n**When to Update This Document**:\n- Before implementing any major architectural change\n- When user feedback contradicts a decision\n- When technical constraints invalidate an option\n\n**Review Frequency**: Monthly or after each major release\n\n**Last Reviewed**: 2025-01-18\n",

 \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Options**: Infinite scroll vs. Load more button vs. Virtual scrolling\n\n**Q3**: Should we preserve the standalone `/tasks/[id]` page for SEO/sharing?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Impact**: Old links break vs. Maintain two views\n\n---\n\n## Future Decisions (Post-MVP)\n\n- **Keyboard Shortcuts**: Define comprehensive shortcut map (Cmd+K, Cmd+/, etc.)\n- **Drag-to-Resize**: Add resizable panel divider\n- **Offline Support**: Handle disconnected state gracefully\n- **Voice Input**: Allow voice-to-text for chat input\n\n---\n\n## Decision Review Process\n\n**When to Update This Document**:\n- Before implementing any major architectural change\n- When user feedback contradicts a decision\n- When technical constraints invalidate an option\n\n**Review Frequency**: Monthly or after each major release\n\n**Last Reviewed**: 2025-01-18\n",
                "after": "# Architecture Decisions Record (ADR)\n\n> **Purpose**: Document key design decisions and their rationale  \n> **Format**: Each decision includes Context, Options Considered, Decision, Consequences  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## Decision Log\n\n### ADR-001: Split-Screen Layout (60/40 Fixed Ratio)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18  \n**Deciders**: Product Team\n\n**Context**:\nUser needs to simultaneously view chat conversation and video content. We evaluated different layout approaches.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Fixed 60/40**: Chat占60%，Video Panel占40%\n2. **Resizable**: 用户可拖拽调整比例\n3. **Preset Layouts**: 提供3个预设（50/50, 60/40, 70/30）\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Fixed 60/40)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Simplifies initial implementation (no drag logic needed)\n- 60/40 ratio tested well in similar tools (Perplexity, Notion AI)\n- Reduces edge cases (min/max width handling)\n- Can upgrade to resizable in v2.1 if needed\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Faster development\n- ✅ Consistent UX across users\n- ❌ Less flexibility for power users\n- ⚠️ May need adjustment based on user feedback\n\n---\n\n### ADR-002: Component Extraction Strategy\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\n`TaskDetailClient.tsx` is 1000+ lines and tightly coupled to the standalone task page. We need to reuse video display logic in the chat panel.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Duplicate Code**: Copy-paste logic into new `VideoDetailPanel`\n2. **Extract Shared Components**: Break `TaskDetailClient` into reusable pieces\n3. **Wrapper Component**: Keep `TaskDetailClient` as-is, wrap it in panel\n\n**Decision**: Option 2 (Extract Shared Components)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Adheres to DRY principle\n- Makes components testable in isolation\n- Easier to maintain (single source of truth)\n- Follows React best practices\n\n**Extraction Plan**:\n```\nTaskDetailClient (monolith)\n  ↓\n  ├── VideoPlayer (extract)\n  ├── SummarySection (extract)\n  ├── ScriptTimeline (already exists)\n  └── MindMapView (extract)\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Reusable components across app\n- ✅ Easier unit testing\n- ❌ Requires refactoring existing code\n- ⚠️ Risk of breaking current task page (mitigated by tests)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-003: Thread-Task Relationship (1:1 Model)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to decide how chat threads relate to video tasks.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **1:1**: One Thread = One Task (focused conversations)\n2. **1:N**: One Thread can contain multiple Tasks (comparison mode)\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (1:1 Model)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Simpler mental model for users\n- Easier to implement (straightforward FK relationship)\n- Aligns with \"Chat about a specific video\" UX\n- Can extend to 1:N later if comparison feature is needed\n\n**Database Design**:\n```sql\nALTER TABLE chat_threads \nADD COLUMN task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id);\n\n-- Optional: Enforce uniqueness\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_chat_threads_task_id_unique \nON chat_threads(task_id) WHERE task_id IS NOT NULL;\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear context (each conversation is about one video)\n- ✅ Simple data model\n- ❌ Users can't compare multiple videos in one chat (can add later)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-004: Library UI (Simple List)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nLibrary sidebar needs to show user's processed videos.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Simple List**: Title + timestamp\n2. **Grouped List**: Group by date (Today, Yesterday, etc.)\n3. **Rich Cards**: Thumbnail + title + summary preview\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Simple List) for MVP\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Minimalist design matches chat-first UX\n- Fast to implement\n- Low cognitive load (focuses on titles)\n- Can upgrade to grouped/rich view later\n\n**Future Enhancement Path**:\n```\nv2.0: Simple list (title + time)\n  ↓\nv2.1: Add date grouping\n  ↓\nv2.2: Add thumbnail preview\n  ↓\nv2.3: Add tag filtering\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clean, fast interface\n- ✅ Easy to scan long lists\n- ❌ Less visual (no thumbnails)\n- ⚠️ May need search for power users with 100+ tasks\n\n---\n\n### ADR-005: Mobile UX (Modal/Drawer Pattern)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nMobile screens (<768px) can't fit split-screen layout.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Stack Layout**: Chat on top, scroll to video details below\n2. **Tab Switching**: [Chat] [Video] tabs\n3. **Modal/Drawer**: Chat as main view, video details in modal\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (Modal/Drawer)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Follows mobile design standards (iOS/Android patterns)\n- Clear hierarchy (chat is primary, video is secondary)\n- No accidental scrolling issues\n- Easy to dismiss (swipe down to close)\n\n**Implementation**:\n```tsx\n// Desktop (≥768px)\n<div className=\"flex\">\n  <ChatContainer />\n  <VideoDetailPanel />\n</div>\n\n// Mobile (<768px)\n<div>\n  <ChatContainer />\n  <Sheet open={isVideoOpen}>\n    <VideoDetailPanel />\n  </Sheet>\n</div>\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Native mobile feel\n- ✅ Consistent with other mobile apps\n- ❌ Requires managing modal state\n- ⚠️ Need smooth animations for good UX\n\n---\n\n### ADR-006: Empty State Design (Guidance + Demo Cards)\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nWhen user first opens chat (no messages), we need to guide them.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Blank Canvas**: Just input box, no guidance\n2. **Onboarding Tutorial**: 3-step walkthrough\n3. **Guidance + Examples**: Text prompt + demo cards\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (Guidance + Examples)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Balances simplicity and discoverability\n- Reuses existing `CommunityTemplates` component\n- Shows product value immediately (via demos)\n- Non-intrusive (no forced tutorial)\n\n**Design**:\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Paste a YouTube URL to start   │\n│  Example: https://youtu.be/...  │\n│                                 │\n│  ────── Community Examples ──── │\n│  [Demo Card 1] [Demo Card 2]    │\n│  [Demo Card 3] [Demo Card 4]    │\n└─────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear call-to-action\n- ✅ Showcases product capabilities\n- ✅ Reduces bounce rate (engagement hook)\n- ❌ Slightly more visual noise\n\n---\n\n### ADR-007: Multi-URL Handling Strategy\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nUsers may paste multiple YouTube URLs in a single message. We need to define how the system handles this scenario.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Process All Simultaneously**: Create multiple tasks in parallel\n2. **Process Separately**: Create multiple threads, one per URL\n3. **Ask User to Choose One**: Only allow one URL per thread\n\n**Decision**: Option 3 (One URL Per Thread)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Enforces clear context (one conversation = one video)\n- Prevents confusion in chat history\n- Aligns with 1:1 Thread-Task relationship (ADR-003)\n- Simpler implementation (no batch processing logic)\n- Users can create multiple threads if needed\n\n**User Experience**:\nWhen multiple URLs detected:\n```\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ ⚠️  Multiple URLs Detected         │\n│                                    │\n│ I can only process one video per   │\n│ conversation. Which one would you  │\n│ like me to analyze?                │\n│                                    │\n│ 1️⃣ https://youtube.com/watch?v=A  │\n│ 2️⃣ https://youtube.com/watch?v=B  │\n│                                    │\n│ Reply with 1 or 2, or paste a      │\n│ single URL to start fresh.         │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Clear, predictable behavior\n- ✅ No ambiguity in chat context\n- ✅ Simpler state management\n- ❌ Users must create separate threads for multiple videos (acceptable)\n\n---\n\n### ADR-008: AI Tool Calling Implementation\n\n**Status**: ✅ Accepted  \n**Date**: 2025-01-18\n\n**Context**:\nNeed to integrate video processing into chat workflow.\n\n**Options Considered**:\n1. **Vercel AI SDK Tools**: Use built-in `tools` parameter\n2. **Custom Function Calling**: Parse messages manually\n3. **Hybrid**: AI SDK for parsing, custom for execution\n\n**Decision**: Option 1 (Vercel AI SDK Tools)\n\n**Rationale**:\n- Native support in AI SDK (`useChat` hook)\n- Handles streaming automatically\n- Standard interface (easier for future LLM swaps)\n- Well-documented patterns\n\n**Implementation**:\n```typescript\nconst { messages } = useChat({\n  api: '/api/chat',\n  tools: {\n    process_video: {\n      description: 'Process a YouTube video',\n      parameters: z.object({\n        video_url: z.string().url(),\n        language: z.string().optional(),\n      }),\n      execute: async ({ video_url, language }) => {\n        // Call backend /api/process-video\n        return { task_id, status }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n})\n```\n\n**Consequences**:\n- ✅ Leverages existing infrastructure\n- ✅ Future-proof (tool calling is LLM standard)\n- ❌ Tied to AI SDK (but acceptable trade-off)\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n**Q1**: How to handle Library pagination for users with 500+ tasks?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Options**: Infinite scroll vs. Load more button vs. Virtual scrolling\n\n**Q2**: Should we preserve the standalone `/tasks/[id]` page for SEO/sharing?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Impact**: Old links break vs. Maintain two views\n\n**Q3**: Should we add \"Compare Videos\" feature in future?  \n**Status**: ⏳ Pending  \n**Note**: Currently enforcing one URL per thread. If users request comparison, we could add a dedicated \"Compare\" mode in v2.1\n\n---\n\n## Future Decisions (Post-MVP)\n\n- **Keyboard Shortcuts**: Define comprehensive shortcut map (Cmd+K, Cmd+/, etc.)\n- **Drag-to-Resize**: Add resizable panel divider\n- **Offline Support**: Handle disconnected state gracefully\n- **Voice Input**: Allow voice-to-text for chat input\n\n---\n\n## Decision Review Process\n\n**When to Update This Document**:\n- Before implementing any major architectural change\n- When user feedback contradicts a decision\n- When technical constraints invalidate an option\n\n**Review Frequency**: Monthly or after each major release\n\n**Last Reviewed**: 2025-01-18\n",

                "before": "# User Flows\n\n> **Purpose**: Complete user journey maps for v2.0  \n> **Format**: Mermaid diagrams + step-by-step descriptions  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Core User Flows\n\n### Flow 1: New User First-Time Experience\n\n**Goal**: User successfully processes their first video\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[Land on /chat] --> B{Authenticated?}\n    B -->|No| C[Redirect to /login]\n    B -->|Yes| D[Show Empty State]\n    \n    D --> E[Display Guidance Text]\n    E --> F[Show Community Demo Cards]\n    \n    F --> G{User Action}\n    G -->|Paste URL| H[User Types YouTube URL]\n    G -->|Click Demo| I[Open Demo in Panel]\n    \n    H --> J[AI Detects URL]\n    J --> K[Show Processing Card]\n    K --> L[Backend Creates Task]\n    L --> M[Realtime Updates Card]\n    M --> N[Card Shows Completed]\n    N --> O[Panel Auto-Opens]\n    O --> P[User Sees Video + Summary]\n    \n    P --> Q[User Asks Follow-up Question]\n    Q --> R[AI Answers Using Context]\n    R --> S[Success: Engaged User]\n```\n\n**Step-by-Step Breakdown**:\n\n1. **Landing** (0s)\n   - URL: `/chat`\n   - UI: Empty chat with guidance text\n   - Action: Read \"Paste a YouTube URL to start...\"\n\n2. **Discover** (5-10s)\n   - UI: Scroll down, see Community Demo Cards\n   - Option A: Click demo → Skip to step 7\n   - Option B: Paste own URL → Continue to step 3\n\n3. **Submission** (10-15s)\n   - User pastes: `https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123`\n   - UI: Input shows URL with preview thumbnail\n   - Action: Press Enter or click Send\n\n4. **Processing Starts** (15-16s)\n   - AI sends message: \"I'll analyze this video for you\"\n   - UI: Shows processing card with spinner\n   - Backend: Creates task, starts workflow\n\n5. **Progress Updates** (16-45s)\n   - Card updates: \"Downloading... (Step 1/4)\"\n   - Card updates: \"Transcribing... (Step 2/4)\"\n   - Card updates: \"Summarizing... (Step 3/4)\"\n   - Realtime: Supabase pushes status changes\n\n6. **Completion** (45-50s)\n   - Card shows: \"✓ Ready\"\n   - Button appears: \"View Summary\"\n   - Panel auto-opens on right side\n\n7. **Exploration** (50s+)\n   - User sees video player + summary tabs\n   - User clicks keypoint → Video seeks to timestamp\n   - User asks: \"What was the main conclusion?\"\n   - AI responds using summary context\n\n**Success Criteria**:\n- ✅ User completes flow in <2 minutes\n- ✅ User sends ≥1 follow-up question\n- ✅ User doesn't encounter errors\n\n---\n\n### Flow 2: Returning User - Access Past Videos\n\n**Goal**: User finds and reopens a previously processed video\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[Open /chat] --> B[See Recent Threads]\n    \n    B --> C{Find Video?}\n    C -->|In Recent| D[Click Thread in List]\n    C -->|Not Recent| E[Click Library Button]\n    \n    E --> F[Library Sidebar Opens]\n    F --> G{Search or Browse?}\n    G -->|Search| H[Type in Search Box]\n    G -->|Browse| I[Scroll Through List]\n    \n    H --> J[Results Filter Live]\n    J --> K[Click Result]\n    \n    I --> K\n    D --> K\n    \n    K --> L[Load Thread Messages]\n    L --> M[Open Video Panel]\n    M --> N[Resume Conversation]\n```\n\n**Step-by-Step**:\n\n1. **Open Chat** (0s)\n   - URL: `/chat`\n   - UI: Shows last active thread (if any)\n\n2. **Trigger Library** (2s)\n   - Click \"☰ Library\" button (or press `Cmd+K`)\n   - Sidebar slides in from left\n\n3. **Find Video** (5-15s)\n   - **Option A - Browse**: Scroll through chronological list\n   - **Option B - Search**: Type title/keyword\n   - List shows: Title + Timestamp + Platform icon\n\n4. **Open** (15s)\n   - Click desired video entry\n   - Library sidebar closes\n   - Thread loads (if exists) OR creates new thread\n   - Panel opens with video details\n\n5. **Continue** (20s+)\n   - User asks new questions about the video\n   - AI has full context from original analysis\n\n---\n\n### Flow 3: Multi-URL Handling\n\n**Goal**: User pastes multiple YouTube URLs, system handles gracefully\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Types Message with 2+ URLs] --> B[Frontend Detects Multiple URLs]\n    B --> C[Show Confirmation Dialog]\n    \n    C --> D{User Choice}\n    D -->|Process Separately| E[Create 2 Tasks + 2 Threads]\n    D -->|Compare Together| F[Create 2 Tasks + 1 Thread]\n    D -->|Cancel| G[Return to Input]\n    \n    E --> H1[Thread 1 Active]\n    E --> H2[Thread 2 Created]\n    H1 --> I[User Can Switch Threads]\n    \n    F --> J[Both Videos in Same Chat]\n    J --> K[AI Can Compare Content]\n```\n\n**Dialog UI**:\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Detected 2 video links:             │\n│ • How AI Works (5:32)               │\n│ • Machine Learning Intro (12:15)    │\n│                                     │\n│ How should I process them?          │\n│ ⚪ Separately (2 conversations)      │\n│ ⚪ Together (compare in one chat)    │\n│                                     │\n│ [Cancel] [Confirm]                  │\n└─────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n### Flow 4: Error Recovery\n\n**Goal**: User encounters error, system guides them to recovery\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Submits URL] --> B[Processing Starts]\n    B --> C{Error Occurs}\n    \n    C -->|Invalid URL| D[Show: URL Not Supported]\n    C -->|Network Fail| E[Show: Connection Lost]\n    C -->|Quota Exceeded| F[Show: Upgrade Prompt]\n    \n    D --> G[Suggest Valid Format]\n    E --> H[Retry Button]\n    F --> I[Link to Pricing]\n    \n    G --> J[User Corrects URL]\n    H --> K[Auto-Retry Backend]\n    I --> L[User Upgrades Plan]\n    \n    J --> M[Success]\n    K --> M\n    L --> M\n```\n\n**Error Messages** (User-Friendly):\n\n| Error Type | Message | Recovery Action |\n|------------|---------|-----------------|\n| Invalid URL | \"This doesn't look like a YouTube URL. Try: https://youtube.com/watch?v=...\" | Show format example |\n| Private Video | \"This video is private. Please check the link and try again.\" | Suggest checking permissions |\n| Network Error | \"Connection lost. Retrying in 3s...\" | Auto-retry 3 times |\n| Quota Exceeded | \"You've used all 5 free videos this month. Upgrade to Pro?\" | Show upgrade CTA |\n| Processing Failed | \"Something went wrong. Our team has been notified. Try again in a few minutes.\" | Manual retry button |\n\n---\n\n## 📱 Mobile-Specific Flows\n\n### Flow 5: Mobile Video Viewing\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User on Mobile] --> B[Paste URL in Chat]\n    B --> C[Processing Card Appears]\n    C --> D[Tap Video Card]\n    \n    D --> E[Sheet Slides Up from Bottom]\n    E --> F[Video Player Fills Screen]\n    F --> G{User Action}\n    \n    G -->|Swipe Down| H[Close Sheet, Back to Chat]\n    G -->|Tap Summary Tab| I[Switch to Summary View]\n    G -->|Ask Question| J[Input Appears at Bottom]\n    \n    J --> K[Question Posted to Chat]\n    K --> H[Sheet Closes, Shows Answer]\n```\n\n**Mobile Gestures**:\n- **Swipe Down**: Close video detail sheet\n- **Swipe Left/Right**: Switch between Summary/Script tabs\n- **Long Press**: Copy text from summary\n- **Double Tap**: Zoom video (if native player)\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Edge Cases & Alternate Paths\n\n### Edge Case 1: Very Long Video (>2 hours)\n\n**Flow**:\n1. User submits 3-hour lecture\n2. System detects duration\n3. Shows warning: \"This video is 3h long. Processing may take 5-10 minutes.\"\n4. User confirms or cancels\n5. If confirmed: Show extended progress bar with estimated time\n\n### Edge Case 2: Unsupported Platform\n\n**Flow**:\n1. User pastes Vimeo URL (not supported yet)\n2. System shows: \"Currently only YouTube, Bilibili, and Xiaoyuzhou are supported.\"\n3. Offers: \"Request support for Vimeo?\"\n4. If yes: Opens feedback form\n\n### Edge Case 3: Duplicate URL in Same Thread\n\n**Flow**:\n1. User pastes URL already processed in current thread\n2. System detects duplicate\n3. Shows: \"You already analyzed this video in this chat. Would you like to open it again?\"\n4. If yes: Opens panel without reprocessing\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Flow Validation Criteria\n\n**Each flow should**:\n- Have ≤5 user actions to complete\n- Provide feedback within 200ms of each action\n- Offer clear recovery path for errors\n- Work identically on desktop and mobile (except gestures)\n\n**Next**: See `ux/11_wireframes.md` for visual layouts of these flows.\n",
                "after": "# User Flows\n\n> **Purpose**: Complete user journey maps for v2.0  \n> **Format**: Mermaid diagrams + step-by-step descriptions  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Core User Flows\n\n### Flow 1: New User First-Time Experience\n\n**Goal**: User successfully processes their first video\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[Land on /chat] --> B{Authenticated?}\n    B -->|No| C[Redirect to /login]\n    B -->|Yes| D[Show Empty State]\n    \n    D --> E[Display Guidance Text]\n    E --> F[Show Community Demo Cards]\n    \n    F --> G{User Action}\n    G -->|Paste URL| H[User Types YouTube URL]\n    G -->|Click Demo| I[Open Demo in Panel]\n    \n    H --> J[AI Detects URL]\n    J --> K[Show Processing Card]\n    K --> L[Backend Creates Task]\n    L --> M[Realtime Updates Card]\n    M --> N[Card Shows Completed]\n    N --> O[Panel Auto-Opens]\n    O --> P[User Sees Video + Summary]\n    \n    P --> Q[User Asks Follow-up Question]\n    Q --> R[AI Answers Using Context]\n    R --> S[Success: Engaged User]\n```\n\n**Step-by-Step Breakdown**:\n\n1. **Landing** (0s)\n   - URL: `/chat`\n   - UI: Empty chat with guidance text\n   - Action: Read \"Paste a YouTube URL to start...\"\n\n2. **Discover** (5-10s)\n   - UI: Scroll down, see Community Demo Cards\n   - Option A: Click demo → Skip to step 7\n   - Option B: Paste own URL → Continue to step 3\n\n3. **Submission** (10-15s)\n   - User pastes: `https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123`\n   - UI: Input shows URL with preview thumbnail\n   - Action: Press Enter or click Send\n\n4. **Processing Starts** (15-16s)\n   - AI sends message: \"I'll analyze this video for you\"\n   - UI: Shows processing card with spinner\n   - Backend: Creates task, starts workflow\n\n5. **Progress Updates** (16-45s)\n   - Card updates: \"Downloading... (Step 1/4)\"\n   - Card updates: \"Transcribing... (Step 2/4)\"\n   - Card updates: \"Summarizing... (Step 3/4)\"\n   - Realtime: Supabase pushes status changes\n\n6. **Completion** (45-50s)\n   - Card shows: \"✓ Ready\"\n   - Button appears: \"View Summary\"\n   - Panel auto-opens on right side\n\n7. **Exploration** (50s+)\n   - User sees video player + summary tabs\n   - User clicks keypoint → Video seeks to timestamp\n   - User asks: \"What was the main conclusion?\"\n   - AI responds using summary context\n\n**Success Criteria**:\n- ✅ User completes flow in <2 minutes\n- ✅ User sends ≥1 follow-up question\n- ✅ User doesn't encounter errors\n\n---\n\n### Flow 2: Returning User - Access Past Videos\n\n**Goal**: User finds and reopens a previously processed video\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[Open /chat] --> B[See Recent Threads]\n    \n    B --> C{Find Video?}\n    C -->|In Recent| D[Click Thread in List]\n    C -->|Not Recent| E[Click Library Button]\n    \n    E --> F[Library Sidebar Opens]\n    F --> G{Search or Browse?}\n    G -->|Search| H[Type in Search Box]\n    G -->|Browse| I[Scroll Through List]\n    \n    H --> J[Results Filter Live]\n    J --> K[Click Result]\n    \n    I --> K\n    D --> K\n    \n    K --> L[Load Thread Messages]\n    L --> M[Open Video Panel]\n    M --> N[Resume Conversation]\n```\n\n**Step-by-Step**:\n\n1. **Open Chat** (0s)\n   - URL: `/chat`\n   - UI: Shows last active thread (if any)\n\n2. **Trigger Library** (2s)\n   - Click \"☰ Library\" button (or press `Cmd+K`)\n   - Sidebar slides in from left\n\n3. **Find Video** (5-15s)\n   - **Option A - Browse**: Scroll through chronological list\n   - **Option B - Search**: Type title/keyword\n   - List shows: Title + Timestamp + Platform icon\n\n4. **Open** (15s)\n   - Click desired video entry\n   - Library sidebar closes\n   - Thread loads (if exists) OR creates new thread\n   - Panel opens with video details\n\n5. **Continue** (20s+)\n   - User asks new questions about the video\n   - AI has full context from original analysis\n\n---\n\n### Flow 3: Multi-URL Detection & Guidance\n\n**Goal**: User pastes multiple YouTube URLs, system guides them to choose one\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Types Message with 2+ URLs] --> B[Frontend Detects Multiple URLs]\n    B --> C[Block Submission]\n    \n    C --> D[Show Warning Message]\n    D --> E{User Action}\n    \n    E -->|Select URL 1| F[AI Processes URL 1]\n    E -->|Select URL 2| G[AI Processes URL 2]\n    E -->|Edit Message| H[User Removes Extra URLs]\n    \n    F --> I[Create Task + Continue Chat]\n    G --> I\n    H --> J[Submit Single URL]\n    J --> I\n```\n\n**AI Response UI**:\n```\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ AI (Just now)                      │\n│                                    │\n│ ⚠️  Multiple URLs Detected         │\n│                                    │\n│ I can only process one video per   │\n│ conversation. Which one would you  │\n│ like me to analyze?                │\n│                                    │\n│ 1️⃣ How AI Works (5:32)             │\n│    https://youtube.com/watch?v=A   │\n│                                    │\n│ 2️⃣ ML Intro (12:15)                │\n│    https://youtube.com/watch?v=B   │\n│                                    │\n│ Reply with 1 or 2, or paste a      │\n│ single URL to start fresh.         │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Alternative: Frontend Prevention**\n```\nInput Box:\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ Paste https://youtube.com/1...     │\n│ https://youtube.com/2...           │\n│                                  📤│\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n                ↓\n        (User clicks Send)\n                ↓\n┌────────────────────────────────────┐\n│ ⚠️ Please remove extra URLs        │\n│ One video per conversation         │\n│                              [OK]  │\n└────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n### Flow 4: Error Recovery\n\n**Goal**: User encounters error, system guides them to recovery\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User Submits URL] --> B[Processing Starts]\n    B --> C{Error Occurs}\n    \n    C -->|Invalid URL| D[Show: URL Not Supported]\n    C -->|Network Fail| E[Show: Connection Lost]\n    C -->|Quota Exceeded| F[Show: Upgrade Prompt]\n    \n    D --> G[Suggest Valid Format]\n    E --> H[Retry Button]\n    F --> I[Link to Pricing]\n    \n    G --> J[User Corrects URL]\n    H --> K[Auto-Retry Backend]\n    I --> L[User Upgrades Plan]\n    \n    J --> M[Success]\n    K --> M\n    L --> M\n```\n\n**Error Messages** (User-Friendly):\n\n| Error Type | Message | Recovery Action |\n|------------|---------|-----------------|\n| Invalid URL | \"This doesn't look like a YouTube URL. Try: https://youtube.com/watch?v=...\" | Show format example |\n| Private Video | \"This video is private. Please check the link and try again.\" | Suggest checking permissions |\n| Network Error | \"Connection lost. Retrying in 3s...\" | Auto-retry 3 times |\n| Quota Exceeded | \"You've used all 5 free videos this month. Upgrade to Pro?\" | Show upgrade CTA |\n| Processing Failed | \"Something went wrong. Our team has been notified. Try again in a few minutes.\" | Manual retry button |\n\n---\n\n## 📱 Mobile-Specific Flows\n\n### Flow 5: Mobile Video Viewing\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[User on Mobile] --> B[Paste URL in Chat]\n    B --> C[Processing Card Appears]\n    C --> D[Tap Video Card]\n    \n    D --> E[Sheet Slides Up from Bottom]\n    E --> F[Video Player Fills Screen]\n    F --> G{User Action}\n    \n    G -->|Swipe Down| H[Close Sheet, Back to Chat]\n    G -->|Tap Summary Tab| I[Switch to Summary View]\n    G -->|Ask Question| J[Input Appears at Bottom]\n    \n    J --> K[Question Posted to Chat]\n    K --> H[Sheet Closes, Shows Answer]\n```\n\n**Mobile Gestures**:\n- **Swipe Down**: Close video detail sheet\n- **Swipe Left/Right**: Switch between Summary/Script tabs\n- **Long Press**: Copy text from summary\n- **Double Tap**: Zoom video (if native player)\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Edge Cases & Alternate Paths\n\n### Edge Case 1: Very Long Video (>2 hours)\n\n**Flow**:\n1. created_at: string\n  updated_at: string\n}\n\nexport interface ChatMessage {\n  id: string\n  thread_id: string\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant'\n  content: string\n  created_at: string\n}\n\nexport interface Task {\n  id: string\n  user_id: string\n  video_url: string\n  video_title: string\n  thumbnail_url?: string\n  status: 'pending' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed'\n  progress: error_message?: string\n}\n\nexport interface MediaController {\n  seek: (seconds: number) => void\n}\n```\n\n**Verification**:\n```bash\n# Check TypeScript compilation\nnpm run build\n# Should complete without errors\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 4: Create Utility Functions\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/lib/url-utils.ts`\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Utility functions for URL detection and validation\n */\n\nconst YOUTUBE_REGEX = /(?:https?:\\/\\/)?(?:www\\.)?(?:youtube\\.com\\/watch\\?v=|youtu\\.be\\/)([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})/g\nconst BILIBILI_REGEX = /(?:https?:\\/\\/)?(?:www\\.)?bilibili\\.com\\/video\\/(BV[a-zA-Z0-9]+)/g\n\n/**\n * Detect all YouTube URLs in a string\n */\nexport function detectYouTubeURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  const matches = text.matchAll(YOUTUBE_REGEX)\n  return Array.from(matches, m => m[0])\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect all Bilibili URLs in a string\n */\nexport function detectBilibiliURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  const matches = text.matchAll(BILIBILI_REGEX)\n  return Array.from(matches, m => m[0])\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect all video URLs (YouTube + Bilibili)\n */\nexport function detectVideoURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  return [\n    ...detectYouTubeURLs(text),\n    ...detectBilibiliURLs(text)\n  ]\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if text contains multiple video URLs\n */\nexport function hasMultipleURLs(text: string): boolean {\n  return detectVideoURLs(text).length > 1\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract video ID from YouTube URL\n */\nexport function extractYouTubeID(url: string): string | null {\n  const match = url.match(YOUTUBE_REGEX)\n  return match ? match[1] : null\n}\n```\n\n**Verification**:\n```bash\n# Create test file\ncat > frontend/src/lib/__tests__/url-utils.test.ts << 'EOF'\nimport { detectVideoURLs, hasMultipleURLs } from '../url-utils'\n\ntest('detects single YouTube URL', () => {\n  const text = \"Check this out: https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123\"\n  expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(1)\n  expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(false)\n})\n\ntest('detects multiple URLs', () => {\n  const text = \"https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123 and https://youtube.com/watch?v=def456\"\n  expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(2)\n  expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(true)\n})\nEOF\n\n# Run tests\nnpm test -- url-utils.test.ts\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 5: Update Supabase Types (if needed)\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/types/database.ts`\n\nAdd or verify chat-related tables:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface // NEW: Link to tasks\n          title: string\n          created_at: string\n          updated_at: string\n        }\n        Insert: {\n          id?: string\n          user_id: string\n          task_id?: string | null\n          title?: string\n          created_at?: string\n          updated_at?: string\n        }\n        Update: {\n          id?: string\n          user_id?: string\n created_at: string\n  updated_at: string\n}\n\nexport interface ChatMessage {\n  id: string\n  thread_id: string\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant'\n  content: string\n  created_at: string\n}\n\nexport complete without errors\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 4: Create Utility Functions\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/lib/url-utils.ts`\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Utility functions for URL detection and validation\n */\n\nconst YOUTUBE_REGEX = /(?:https?:\\/\\/)?(?:www\\.)?(?:youtube\\.com\\/watch\\?v=|youtu\\.be\\/)([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})/g\nconst BILIBILI_REGEX = /(?:https?:\\/\\/)?(?:www\\.)?bilibili\\.com\\/video\\/(BV[a-zA-Z0-9]+)/g\n\n/**\n * Detect all YouTube URLs in a string\n */\nexport function detectYouTubeURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  const matches = text.matchAll(YOUTUBE_REGEX)\n  return Array.from(matches, m => m[0])\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect all Bilibili URLs in a string\n */\nexport function detectBilibiliURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  const matches = text.matchAll(BILIBILI_REGEX)\n  return Array.from(matches, m => m[0])\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect all video URLs (YouTube + Bilibili)\n */\nexport function detectVideoURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  return [\n    ...detectYouTubeURLs(text),\n    ...detectBilibiliURLs(text)\n  ]\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if text contains multiple video URLs\n */\nexport function hasMultipleURLs(text: string): boolean {\n  return detectVideoURLs(text).length > 1\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract video ID from YouTube URL\n */\nexport function extractYouTubeID(url: string): string | null {\n  const match = url.match(YOUTUBE_REGEX)\n  return match ? match[1] : null\n}\n```\n\n**Verification**:\n```bash\n# Create test file\ncat > frontend/src/lib/__tests__/url-utils.test.ts << 'EOF'\nimport { detectVideoURLs, hasMultipleURLs } from '../url-utils'\n\ntest('detects single YouTube URL', () => {\n  const text = \"Check this out: https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123\"\n  expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(1)\n  expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(false)\n})\n\ntest('detects multiple URLs', () => {\n  const text = \"https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123 and https://youtube.com/watch?v=def456\"\n  expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(2)\n  expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(true)\n})\nEOF\n\n# Run tests\nnpm test -- url-utils.test.ts\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 5: Update Supabase Types (if needed)\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/types/database.ts`\n\nAdd or verify chat-related tables:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface task_id?: string | null\n          title?: string\n          created_at?: string\n          updated_at?: string\n        }\n        Update: {\n          id?: string\n          user_id?: string\n Task 2.0 (Setup Workspace)\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 Objective\n\nCreate the root ChatWorkspace container that manages the split-screen layout, state coordination between chat and video panel, and responsive behavior.\n\n---\n\n## 📋 Prerequisites\n\n- [x] Task 2.0 completed\n- [x] Directory structure created (`components/chat/`)\n- [x] Type definitions exist (`types/chat.ts`)\n- [x] URL utilities created (`lib/url-utils.ts`)\n\n**Verification Command**:\n```bash\ncd frontend/src\nls components/chat/\nls types/chat.ts\nls lib/url-utils.ts\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🔨 Implementation Steps\n\n### Step 1: Create ChatWorkspace Component\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatWorkspace.tsx`\n\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useState, useEffect } from 'react'\nimport { useSearchParams, useRouter } from 'next/navigation'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\nimport { ChatContainer } from './ChatContainer'\nimport { VideoDetailPanel } from './VideoDetailPanel'\nimport { LibrarySidebar } from './LibrarySidebar'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { Menu, X } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function ChatWorkspace() {\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams()\n  const router = useRouter()\n  const taskId = searchParams.get('task')\n  \n  // State management\n  const [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(!!taskId)\n  const [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(taskId)\n  const [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\n  const [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n\n  // Detect mobile viewport\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const checkMobile = () => setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 1024)\n    checkMobile()\n    window.addEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n  }, [])\n\n  // Sync with URL params\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (taskId && taskId !== currentTaskId) {\n      setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n      setIsPanelOpen(true)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  // Handle task creation from chat\n  const handleTaskCreated = (newTaskId: string) => {\n    setCurrentTaskId(newTaskId)\n    setIsPanelOpen(true)\n    \n    // Update URL without navigation\n    const params = new URLSearchParams(searchParams.toString())\n    params.set('task', newTaskId)\n    router.push(`?${params.toString()}`, { scroll: false })\n  }\n\n  // Handle panel close\n  const handleClosePanel = () => {\n    setIsPanelOpen(false)\n    \n    // Remove task param from URL\n    const params = new URLSearchParams(searchParams.toString())\n    params.delete('task')\n    const newUrl = params.toString() ? `?${params.toString()}` : ''\n    router.push(newUrl, { scroll: false })\n  }\n\n  // Handle task selection from library\n  const handleSelectTask = (taskId: string) => {\n    setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n    setIsPanelOpen(true)\n    setIsLibraryOpen(false)\n    \n    // Update URL\n    const params = new URLSearchParams(searchParams.toString())\n    params.set('task', taskId)\n    router.push(`?${params.toString()}`, { scroll: false })\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"h-screen flex flex-col bg-background\">\n      {/* Top Bar */}\n      <header className=\"h-16 border-b border-white/10 flex items-center px-4 shrink-0\">\n        <Button\n          variant=\"ghost\"\n          size=\"sm\"\n          onClick={() => setIsLibraryOpen(true)}\n          className=\"gap-2\"\n        >\n          <Menu className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n          <span className=\"hidden md:inline\">Library</span>\n        </Button>\n\n        <div className=\"flex-1\" />\n\n        {/* Close panel button (desktop only, when panel is open) */}\n        {!isMobile && isPanelOpen && (\n          <Button\n            variant=\"ghost\"\n            size=\"sm\"\n            onClick={handleClosePanel}\n            className=\"gap-2\"\n          >\n            <X className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n            Close Panel\n          </Button>\n        )}\n      </header>\n\n      {/* Main Content Area */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 flex overflow-hidden\">\n        {/* Chat Container (Left Side) */}\n        <ChatContainer\n          className={cn(\n            \"flex-1 min-w-[320px]\",\n            // On desktop, limit width when panel is open\n            !isMobile && isPanelOpen && \"max-w-[60%]\"\n          )}\n          onTaskCreated={handleTaskCreated}\n          onOpenPanel={(taskId) => {\n            setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n            setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          }}\n        />\n\n        {/* Video Detail Panel (Right Side - Desktop Only) */}\n        {!isMobile && (\n          <div\n            className={cn(\n              \"transition-all duration-300 ease-out border-l border-white/10 bg-background\",\n              isPanelOpen ? \"w-[40%] opacity-100\" : \"w-0 opacity-0 overflow-hidden\"\n            )}\n          >\n            {currentTaskId && isPanelOpen && (\n              <VideoDetailPanel\n                taskId={currentTaskId}\n                onClose={handleClosePanel}\n              />\n            )}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Library Sidebar (Slide-out from left) */}\n      <LibrarySidebar\n        isOpen={isLibraryOpen}\n        onClose={() => setIsLibraryOpen(false)}\n        onSelectTask={handleSelectTask}\n      />\n\n      {/* Mobile Video Modal */}\n      {isMobile && currentTaskId && isPanelOpen && (\n        <div className=\"fixed inset-0 z-50 bg-background\">\n          <VideoDetailPanel\n            taskId={currentTaskId}\n            onClose={() => setIsPanelOpen(false)}\n          />\n        </div>\n      )}\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n**Verification**:\n```bash\n# Check TypeScript compilation\ncd frontend\nnpx tsc --noEmit\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: Create ChatContainer Component (Stub)\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatContainer.tsx`\n\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\n\ninterface ChatContainerProps {\n  className?: string\n  onTaskCreated?: (taskId: string) => void\n  onOpenPanel?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n\nexport function ChatContainer({\n  className,\n  onTaskCreated,\n  onOpenPanel\n}: ChatContainerProps) {\n  return (\n    <div          ChatContainer placeholder\n          <br />\n          <small>Will be implemented in Task 2.4</small>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Input Area */}\n      <div className=\"p-4 border-t border-white/10\">\n        <input\n          type=\"text\"\n          placeholder=\"Paste a YouTube URL to start...\"\n          className=\"w-full px-4 py-2 bg-white/5 border e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k') {\n      e.preventDefault()\n      setIsLibraryOpen(true)\n    }\n\n    // Escape to close panel (desktop only)\n    if (e.key === 'Escape' && !isMobile && isPanelOpen) {\n      handleClosePanel()\n    }\n  }\n\n  window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown)\n  return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown)\n}, [isMobile, isPanelOpen])\n```\n\n**Verification**:\n```bash\n# Test in browser\n# Press Cmd+K → Library should open\n# Press Escape (with panel open) → Panel should close\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Validation Create test file\n00209| cat > frontend/src/lib/__tests__/url-utils.test.ts << 'EOF'\n00210| import { detectVideoURLs, hasMultipleURLs } from '../url-utils'\n00211| \n00212| test('detects single YouTube URL', () => {\n00213|   const text = \"Check this out: https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123\"\n00214|   expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(1)\n00215|   expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(false)\n00216| })\n00217| \n00218| test('detects multiple URLs', () => {\n00219|   const text = \"https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123 and https://youtube.com/watch?v=def456\"\n00220|   expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(2)\n00221|   expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(true)\n00222| })\n00223| EOF\n00224| \n00225| # Run tests\n00226| npm test -- url-utils.test.ts\n00227| ```\n00228| \n00229| ---\n00230| \n00231| ### Step 5: Update Supabase Types (if needed)\n00232| \n00233| **File**: `frontend/src/types/database.ts`\n00234| \n00235| Add or verify chat-related tables:\n00236| \n00237| ```typescript\n00238| export interface Database {\n00239|   public: {\n00240|     Tables: {\n00241|       // ... existing tables\n00242|       \n00243|       chat_threads: {\n00244|         Row: {\n00245|           id: string\n00246|           user_id: string\n00247|           task_id: string | null  // NEW: Link to tasks\n00248|           title: string\n00249|           created_at: string\n00250|           updated_at: string\n00251|         }\n00252|         Insert: {\n00253|           id?: string\n00254|           user_id: string\n00255|           task_id?: string | null\n00256|           title?: string\n00257|           created_at?: string\n00258|           updated_at?: string\n00259|         }\n00260|         Update: {\n00261|           id?: string\n00262|           user_id?: string\n00263|           task_id?: string | null\n00264|           title?: string\n00265|           updated_at?: string\n00266|         }\n00267|       }\n00268|       \n00269|       // ... other tables\n00270|     }\n00271|   }\n00272| }\n00273| ```\n00274| \n00275| ---\n00276| \n00277| ## ✅ Validation Checklist\n00278| \n00279| Before proceeding to Task 2.1, verify:\n00280| \n00281| - [ ] All directories created (`chat/`, `chat/messages/`, `tasks/shared/`)\n00282| - [ ] Type definitions file created (`types/chat.ts`)\n00283| - [ ] URL utilities created (`lib/url-utils.ts`)\n00284| - [ ] All dependencies installed (check `package.json`)\n00285| - [ ] TypeScript compilation succeeds (`npm run build`)\n00286| - [ ] No console errors in dev server (`npm run dev`)\n00287| \n00288| **Final Verification**:\n00289| ```bash\n00290| # Integrates with AI SDK for message handling.\n00170| \n00171| ### Props Interface\n00172| ```typescript\n00173| interface ChatContainerProps {\n00174|   className?: string\n00175|   onTaskCreated?: (taskId: string) => void\n00176|   onOpenPanel?: (taskId: string) => void\n00177| }\n00178| ```\n00179| \n00180| ### Implementation Template\n00181| ```typescript\n00182| 'use client'\n00183| \n00184| import { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'\n00185| import { useState } from 'react'\n00186| import { ChatMessages } from './ChatMessages'\n00187| import { ChatInput } from './ChatInput'\n00188| \n00189| export function ChatContainer({ \n00190|   className, \n00191|   onTaskCreated,\n00192|   onOpenPanel \n00193| }: ChatContainerProps) {\n00194|   const { messages, sendMessage, status } = useChat({\n00195|     api: '/api/chat',\n00196|     onFinish: (message) => {\n00197|       // Check if message contains task ID\n00198|       const taskId = extractTaskId(message)\n00199|       if (taskId && onTaskCreated) {\n00200|         onTaskCreated(taskId)\n00201|       }\n00202|     }\n00203|   })\n00204| \n00205|   const handleSubmit = async (content: string) => {\n00206|     await sendMessage({ text: content })\n00207|   }\n00208| \n00209|   return (\n00210|     <div className={cn(\"flex flex-col h-full\", className)}>\n00211|       <ChatMessages \n00212|         messages={messages}\n00213|         onOpenPanel={onOpenPanel}\n00214|       />\n00215|       <ChatInput \n00216|         onSubmit={handleSubmit}\n00217|         disabled={status === 'streaming'}\n00218|       />\n00219|     </div>\n00220|   )\n00221| }\n00222| \n00223| function extractTaskId(message: any): string | null {\n00224|   // Parse message for task_id in tool call result\n00225|   // Implementation depends on AI SDK message format\n00226|   return null\n00227| }\n00228| ```\n00229| \n00230| ---\n00231| \n00232| ## 3.
