ChatWorkspace\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatWorkspace.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRoot container for the split-screen chat interface. Manages layout and state coordination between chat and video panel.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatWorkspaceProps {\n  // No props - uses URL params and context\n}\n```\n\n### Local State\n```typescript\nconst [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(false)\nconst [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(null)\nconst [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\nconst [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useState, useEffect } from 'react'\nimport { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\nimport { ChatContainer } from './ChatContainer'\nimport { VideoDetailPanel } from './VideoDetailPanel'\nimport { LibrarySidebar } from './LibrarySidebar'\n\nexport function ChatWorkspace() {\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams()\n  const taskId = searchParams.get('task')\n  \n  const [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(!!taskId)\n  const [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(taskId)\n  const [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\n  const [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n\n  // Detect mobile\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const checkMobile = () => setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 768)\n    checkMobile()\n    window.addEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n  }, [])\n\n  // Open panel when task ID changes\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (taskId) {\n      setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n      setIsPanelOpen(true)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  const handleTaskCreated = (newTaskId: string) => {\n    setCurrentTaskId(newTaskId)\n    setIsPanelOpen(true)\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"h-screen flex flex-col\">\n      {/* Top Bar */}\n      <header className=\"h-16 border-b border-white/10 flex items-center px-4 shrink-0\">\n        <button \n          onClick={() => setIsLibraryOpen(true)}\n          className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"\n        >\n          ☰ Library\n        </button>\n        <div className=\"flex-1\" />\n        {/* User menu */}\n      </header>\n\n      {/* Main Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 flex overflow-hidden\">\n        <ChatContainer \n          className=\"flex-1 min-w-[400px]\"\n          onTaskCreated={handleTaskCreated}\n          onOpenPanel={(taskId) => {\n            setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n            setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          }}\n        />\n\n        {/* Desktop Panel */}\n        {!isMobile && (\n          <div \n            className={cn(\n              \"transition-all duration-300 border-l border-white/10 bg-background\",\n              isPanelOpen ? \"w-[40%]\" : \"w-0 opacity-0 overflow-hidden\"\n            )}\n          >\n            {currentTaskId && (\n              <VideoDetailPanel \n                taskId={currentTaskId}\n                onClose={() => setIsPanelOpen(false)}\n              />\n            )}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Library Sidebar */}\n      <LibrarySidebar \n        isOpen={isLibraryOpen}\n        onClose={() => setIsLibraryOpen(false)}\n        onSelectTask={(taskId) => {\n          setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n          setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          setIsLibraryOpen(false)\n        }}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n### Validation\n- [ ] Renders split-screen on desktop (≥1024px)\n- [ ] Panel animates smoothly (300ms)\n- [ ] URL param `?task=xxx` auto-opens panel\n- [ ] Library sidebar opens with `Cmd+K`\n\n---\n\n## 2. ChatContainer\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatContainer.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nContains chat messages and input. Integrates with AI SDK for message handling.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatContainerProps {\n  className?: string\n  onTaskCreated?: (taskId: string) => void\n  onOpenPanel?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'\nimport { useState } from 'react'\nimport { ChatMessages } from './ChatMessages'\nimport { ChatInput } from './ChatInput'\n\nexport function ChatContainer({ \n  className, \n  onTaskCreated,\n  onOpenPanel \n}: ChatContainerProps) {\n  const { messages, sendMessage, status } = useChat({\n    api: '/api/chat',\n    onFinish: (message) => {\n      // Check if message contains task ID\n      const taskId = extractTaskId(message)\n      if (taskId && onTaskCreated) {\n        onTaskCreated(taskId)\n      }\n    }\n  })\n\n  const handleSubmit = async (content: string) => {\n    await sendMessage({ text: content })\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"flex flex-col h-full\", className)}>\n      <ChatMessages \n        messages={messages}\n        onOpenPanel={onOpenPanel}\n      />\n      <ChatInput \n        onSubmit={handleSubmit}\n        disabled={status === 'streaming'}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n\nfunction extractTaskId(message: any): string | null {\n  // Parse message for task_id in tool call result\n  // Implementation depends on AI SDK message format\n  return null\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. VideoCardMessage\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/messages/VideoCardMessage.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nDisplays video processing status as a card in chat messages.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoCardMessageProps {\n  taskId: string\n  videoUrl: string\n  title?: string\n  thumbnailUrl?: string\n  status: 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed'\n  progress?: number\n  onViewClick?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { Card } from '@/components/ui/card'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress'\nimport { Loader2, CheckCircle, XCircle } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function VideoCardMessage({\n  taskId,\n  videoUrl,\n  title,\n  thumbnailUrl,\n  status,\n  progress = 0,\n  onViewClick\n}: VideoCardMessageProps) {\n  const StatusIcon = {\n    processing: Loader2,\n    completed: CheckCircle,\n    failed: XCircle\n  }[status]\n\n  return (\n    <Card className=\"p-4 max-w-sm\">\n      {/* Thumbnail */}\n      {thumbnailUrl && (\n        <img \n          src={thumbnailUrl} \n          alt={title}\n          className=\"w-full aspect-video object-cover rounded-lg mb-3\"\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Title */}\n      <h3 className=\"font-semibold mb-2 line-clamp-2\">\n        {title || 'Processing video...'}\n      </h3>\n\n      {/* Status */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-3\">\n        <StatusIcon className={cn(\n          \"w-4 h-4\",\n          status === 'processing' && \"animate-spin\",\n          status === 'completed' && \"text-green-500\",\n          status === 'failed' && \"text-red-500\"\n        )} />\n        <span>\n          {status === 'processing' && `Processing (${progress}%)`}\n          {status === 'completed' && 'Ready'}\n          {status === 'failed' && 'Failed'}\n        </span>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Progress Bar */}\n      {status === 'processing' && (\n        <Progress value={progress} className=\"mb-3\" />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Action Button */}\n      {status === 'completed' && onViewClick && (\n        <Button \n          onClick={() => onViewClick(taskId)}\n          className=\"w-full\"\n        >\n          View Summary\n        </Button>\n      )}\n    </Card>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. VideoDetailPanel\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/VideoDetailPanel.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRight-side panel displaying video player and analysis results. Reuses extracted components from TaskDetailClient.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoDetailPanelProps {\n  taskId: string\n  onClose?: () => void\n  className?: string\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { VideoPlayer } from '@/components/tasks/VideoPlayer'\nimport { SummarySection } from '@/components/tasks/SummarySection'\nimport { ScriptTimeline } from '@/components/tasks/ScriptTimeline'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { X } from 'lucide-react'\nimport { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs'\n\nexport function VideoDetailPanel({ \n  taskId, \n  onClose, \n  className \n}: VideoDetailPanelProps) {\n  const [task, setTask] = useState(null)\n  const [outputs, setOutputs] = useState([])\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    // Fetch task and outputs\n    const fetchData = async () => {\n      const { data: taskData } = await supabase\n        .from('tasks')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('id', taskId)\n        .single()\n      \n      const { data: outputsData } = await supabase\n        .from('task_outputs')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('task_id', taskId)\n\n      setTask(taskData)\n      setOutputs(outputsData || [])\n    }\n\n    fetchData()\n\n    // Realtime subscription\n    const channel = supabase.channel(`task_${taskId}`)\n      .on('postgres_changes', {\n        event: '*',\n        schema: 'public',\n        table: 'tasks',\n        filter: `id=eq.${taskId}`\n      }, (payload) => {\n        setTask(payload.new)\n      })\n      .subscribe()\n\n    return () => {\n      supabase.removeChannel(channel)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  if (!task) return <div className=\"p-4\">Loading...</div>\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"h-full flex flex-col\", className)}>\n      {/* Header */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-b border-white/10\">\n        <h2 className=\"font-semibold truncate\">{task.video_title}</h2>\n        {onClose && (\n          <Button variant=\"ghost\" size=\"icon\" onClick={onClose}>\n            <X className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n          </Button>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4\">\n        <VideoPlayer \n          videoUrl={task.video_url}\n          title={task.video_title}\n          coverUrl={task.thumbnail_url}\n        />\n\n        <Tabs defaultValue=\"summary\">\n          <TabsList>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"summary\">Summary</TabsTrigger>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"script\">Script</TabsTrigger>\n          </TabsList>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"summary\">\n            <SummarySection \n              outputs={outputs}\n              taskTitle={task.video_title}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"script\">\n            <ScriptTimeline \n              scriptRawContent={outputs.find(o => o.kind === 'script_raw')?.content}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n        </Tabs>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. LibrarySidebar\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/LibrarySidebar.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nSlide-out sidebar showing user's task history.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface LibrarySidebarProps {\n  isOpen: boolean\n  onClose: () => void\n  onSelectTask: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { Sheet, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetTitle } from '@/components/ui/sheet'\nimport { Input } from '@/components/ui/input'\nimport { Search } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function LibrarySidebar({ \n  isOpen, \n  onClose, \n  onSelectTask \n}: LibrarySidebarProps) {\n  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState([])\n  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('')\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (isOpen) {\n      fetchTasks()\n    }\n  }, [isOpen])\n\n  const fetchTasks = async () => {\n    const { data } = await supabase\n      .from('tasks')\n      .select('*')\n      .eq('status', 'completed')\n      .order('created_at', { ascending: false })\n      .limit(20)\n\n    setTasks(data || [])\n  }\n\n  const filteredTasks = tasks.filter(task =>\n    task.video_title?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())\n  )\n\n  return (\n    <Sheet open={isOpen} onOpenChange={onClose}>\n      <SheetContent side=\"left\" className=\"w-[320px] p-0\">\n        <SheetHeader className=\"p-4 border-b\">\n          <SheetTitle>Library</SheetTitle>\n        </SheetHeader>\n\n        {/* Search */}\n        <div className=\"p-4\">\n          <div className=\"relative\">\n            <Search className=\"absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-muted-foreground\" />\n            <Input\n              placeholder=\"Search videos...\"\n              value={searchQuery}\n              onChange={(e) => setSearchQuery(e.target.value)}\n              className=\"pl-9\"\n            />\n          </div>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* Task List */}\n        <div className=\"overflow-y-auto\">\n          {filteredTasks.map(task => (\n            <button\n              key={task.id}\n              onClick={() => {\n                onSelectTask(task.id)\n                onClose()\n              }}\n              className=\"w-full text-left p-4 hover:bg-white/5 transition-colors border-b border-white/5\"\n            >\n              <div className=\"font-medium truncate\">{task.video_title}</div>\n              <div className=\"text-sm text-muted-foreground\">\n                {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n              </div>\n            </button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      </SheetContent>\n    </Sheet>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Component Checklist\n\n**Before marking any component as complete**:\n\n- [ ] TypeScript types defined and exported\n- [ ] Props documented with JSDoc comments\n- Manages layout and state coordination between chat and video panel.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatWorkspaceProps {\n  // No props - uses URL params and context\n}\n```\n\n### return (\n    <div className=\"h-screen flex flex-col\">\n      {/* Top Bar */}\n      <header className=\"h-16 border-b border-white/10 flex items-center px-4 shrink-0\">\n        <button \n onClose={() => setIsPanelOpen(false)}\n              />\n            )}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Library Sidebar */}\n      <LibrarySidebar \n ChatContainer\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatContainer.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nContains chat messages and input. Integrates with AI SDK **v6** for message handling.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatContainerProps {\n  className?: string\n  onTaskCreated?: (taskId: string) => void\n  onOpenPanel?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template (AI SDK v6)\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'  // ⚠️ MUST use @ai-sdk/react (v6)\nimport { useState } from 'react'\nimport { ChatMessages } from './ChatMessages'\nimport { ChatInput } from './ChatInput'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\n\nexport function ChatContainer({ \n  className, \n  onTaskCreated,\n  onOpenPanel \n}: ChatContainerProps) {\n  // AI SDK v6 useChat hook\n  const { messages, append, isLoading, error } = useChat({\n    api: '/api/chat',\n    onFinish: (message) => {\n      // Check if message contains task ID\n      const taskId = extractTaskId(message)\n      if (taskId && onTaskCreated) {\n        onTaskCreated(taskId)\n      }\n    }\n  })\n\n  const handleSubmit = async (content: string) => {\n    // v6 API: use append() to add user message\n    await append({ role: 'user', content })\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"flex flex-col h-full\", className)}>\n      <ChatMessages \n        messages={messages}\n        onOpenPanel={onOpenPanel}\n      />\n      <ChatInput \n        onSubmit={handleSubmit}\n        disabled={isLoading}\n        error={error?.message}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n\nfunction extractTaskId(message: any): string | null {\n  // Parse message for task_id in tool call result\n  // Implementation depends on AI SDK message format\n  return null\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. VideoCardMessage\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/messages/VideoCardMessage.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nDisplays video processing status as a card in chat messages.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoCardMessageProps {\n  taskId: string\n  videoUrl: string\n  title?: string\n  thumbnailUrl?: string\n  status: 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed'\n  progress?: number\n  onViewClick?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { Card } from '@/components/ui/card'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress'\nimport { Loader2, CheckCircle, XCircle } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function VideoCardMessage({\n  taskId,\n  videoUrl,\n  title,\n  thumbnailUrl,\n  status,\n  progress = 0,\n  onViewClick\n}: VideoCardMessageProps) {\n  const StatusIcon = {\n    processing: Loader2,\n    completed: CheckCircle,\n    failed: XCircle\n  }[status]\n\n  return (\n    <Card className=\"p-4 max-w-sm\">\n      {/* Thumbnail */}\n      {thumbnailUrl && (\n        <img \n          src={thumbnailUrl} \n          alt={title}\n          className=\"w-full aspect-video object-cover rounded-lg mb-3\"\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Title */}\n      <h3 className=\"font-semibold mb-2 line-clamp-2\">\n        {title || 'Processing video...'}\n      </h3>\n\n      {/* Status */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-3\">\n        <StatusIcon className={cn(\n          \"w-4 h-4\",\n          status === 'processing' && \"animate-spin\",\n          status === 'completed' && \"text-green-500\",\n          status === 'failed' && \"text-red-500\"\n        )} />\n        <span>\n          {status === 'processing' && `Processing (${progress}%)`}\n          {status === 'completed' && 'Ready'}\n          {status === 'failed' && 'Failed'}\n        </span>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Progress Bar */}\n      {status === 'processing' && (\n        <Progress value={progress} className=\"mb-3\" />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Action Button */}\n      {status === 'completed' && onViewClick && (\n        <Button \n          onClick={() => onViewClick(taskId)}\n          className=\"w-full\"\n        >\n          View Summary\n        </Button>\n      )}\n    </Card>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. VideoDetailPanel\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/VideoDetailPanel.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRight-side panel displaying video player and analysis results. Reuses extracted components from TaskDetailClient.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoDetailPanelProps {\n  taskId: string\n  onClose?: () => void\n  className?: string\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { VideoPlayer } from '@/components/tasks/VideoPlayer'\nimport { SummarySection } from '@/components/tasks/SummarySection'\nimport { ScriptTimeline } from '@/components/tasks/ScriptTimeline'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { X } from 'lucide-react'\nimport { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs'\n\nexport function VideoDetailPanel({ \n  taskId, \n  onClose, \n  className \n}: VideoDetailPanelProps) {\n  const [task, setTask] = useState(null)\n  const [outputs, setOutputs] = useState([])\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    // Fetch task and outputs\n    const fetchData = async () => {\n      const { data: taskData } = await supabase\n        .from('tasks')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('id', taskId)\n        .single()\n      \n      const { data: outputsData } = await supabase\n        .from('task_outputs')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('task_id', taskId)\n\n      setTask(taskData)\n      setOutputs(outputsData || [])\n    }\n\n    fetchData()\n\n    // Realtime subscription\n    const channel = supabase.channel(`task_${taskId}`)\n      .on('postgres_changes', {\n        event: '*',\n        schema: 'public',\n        table: 'tasks',\n        filter: `id=eq.${taskId}`\n      }, (payload) => {\n        setTask(payload.new)\n      })\n      .subscribe()\n\n    return () => {\n      supabase.removeChannel(channel)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  if (!task) return <div className=\"p-4\">Loading...</div>\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"h-full flex flex-col\", className)}>\n      {/* Header */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-b border-white/10\">\n        <h2 className=\"font-semibold truncate\">{task.video_title}</h2>\n        {onClose && (\n          <Button variant=\"ghost\" size=\"icon\" onClick={onClose}>\n            <X className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n          </Button>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4\">\n        <VideoPlayer \n          videoUrl={task.video_url}\n          title={task.video_title}\n          coverUrl={task.thumbnail_url}\n        />\n\n        <Tabs defaultValue=\"summary\">\n          <TabsList>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"summary\">Summary</TabsTrigger>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"script\">Script</TabsTrigger>\n          </TabsList>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"summary\">\n            <SummarySection \n              outputs={outputs}\n              taskTitle={task.video_title}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"script\">\n            <ScriptTimeline \n              scriptRawContent={outputs.find(o => o.kind === 'script_raw')?.content}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n        </Tabs>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. LibrarySidebar\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/LibrarySidebar.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nSlide-out sidebar showing user's task history.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface LibrarySidebarProps {\n  isOpen: boolean\n  onClose: () => void\n  onSelectTask: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { Sheet, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetTitle } from '@/components/ui/sheet'\nimport { Input } from '@/components/ui/input'\nimport { Search } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function LibrarySidebar({ \n  isOpen, \n  onClose, \n  onSelectTask \n}: LibrarySidebarProps) {\n  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState([])\n  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('')\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (isOpen) {\n      fetchTasks()\n    }\n  }, [isOpen])\n\n  const fetchTasks = async () => {\n    const { data } = await supabase\n      .from('tasks')\n      .select('*')\n      .eq('status', 'completed')\n      .order('created_at', { ascending: false })\n      .limit(20)\n\n    setTasks(data || [])\n  }\n\n  const filteredTasks = tasks.filter(task =>\n    task.video_title?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())\n  )\n\n  return (\n    <Sheet open={isOpen} onOpenChange={onClose}>\n      <SheetContent side=\"left\" className=\"w-[320px] p-0\">\n        <SheetHeader className=\"p-4 border-b\">\n          <SheetTitle>Library</SheetTitle>\n        </SheetHeader>\n\n        {/* Search */}\n        <div className=\"p-4\">\n          <div className=\"relative\">\n            <Search className=\"absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-muted-foreground\" />\n            <Input\n              placeholder=\"Search videos...\"\n              value={searchQuery}\n              onChange={(e) => setSearchQuery(e.target.value)}\n              className=\"pl-9\"\n            />\n          </div>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* Task List */}\n        <div className=\"overflow-y-auto\">\n          {filteredTasks.map(task => (\n            <button\n              key={task.id}\n              onClick={() => {\n                onSelectTask(task.id)\n                onClose()\n              }}\n              className=\"w-full text-left p-4 hover:bg-white/5 transition-colors border-b border-white/5\"\n            >\n              <div className=\"font-medium truncate\">{task.video_title}</div>\n              <div className=\"text-sm text-muted-foreground\">\n                {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n              </div>\n            </button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      </SheetContent>\n    </Sheet>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Component Checklist\n\n**Before marking any component as complete**:\n\n- [ ] TypeScript types defined and exported\n- [ ] Props documented with JSDoc comments\n- All implementations MUST use v6 APIs.\n\n```bash\n# Required packages (v6 ONLY)\nnpm install ai@^4.0.0 @ai-sdk/react@^1.0.0 @ai-sdk/openai@^1.0.0\n```\n\n**v6 Import Pattern**:\n```typescript\n// ✅ CORRECT - AI SDK v6\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'\n\n// ❌ WRONG - Old v3 pattern (DO NOT USE)\nimport { useChat } from 'ai/react'\n```\n\n**Key v6 Changes**:\n- `useChat` returns `{ messages, sendMessage, status }` (not `handleSubmit`)\n- Use `sendMessage({ content: string })` instead of form submission\n- `status` values: `'idle' | 'streaming' | 'error'`\n\n---\n\n## 📦 Component Hierarchy\n\n```\nChatWorkspace (New - Root Container)\n├── ChatContainer (New)\n│   ├── ChatMessages (Refactor AssistantChat)\n│   ├── MessageItem (New)\n│   │   ├── VideoCardMessage (New)\n│   │   ├── TextMessage (Exists)\n│   │   └── ProcessingMessage (New)\n│   └── ChatInput (New)\n│\n├── VideoDetailPanel (New)\n│   ├── VideoPlayer (Extract from TaskDetailClient)\n│   ├── SummarySection (Extract from TaskDetailClient)\n│   ├── ScriptTimeline (Reuse existing)\n│   └── MindMapView (Extract from TaskDetailClient)\n│\n└── LibrarySidebar (New)\n    ├── LibrarySearch (New)\n    └── TaskListItem (New)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 1. ChatWorkspace\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatWorkspace.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRoot container for the split-screen chat interface. Manages layout and state coordination between chat and video panel.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatWorkspaceProps {\n  // No props - uses URL params and context\n}\n```\n\n### Local State\n```typescript\nconst [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(false)\nconst [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(null)\nconst [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\nconst [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useState, useEffect } from 'react'\nimport { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\nimport { ChatContainer } from './ChatContainer'\nimport { VideoDetailPanel } from './VideoDetailPanel'\nimport { LibrarySidebar } from './LibrarySidebar'\n\nexport function ChatWorkspace() {\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams()\n  const taskId = searchParams.get('task')\n  \n  const [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(!!taskId)\n  const [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(taskId)\n  const [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\n  const [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n\n  // Detect mobile\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const checkMobile = () => setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 768)\n    checkMobile()\n    window.addEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n  }, [])\n\n  // Open panel when task ID changes\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (taskId) {\n      setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n      setIsPanelOpen(true)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  const handleTaskCreated = (newTaskId: string) => {\n    setCurrentTaskId(newTaskId)\n    setIsPanelOpen(true)\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"h-screen flex flex-col\">\n      {/* Top Bar */}\n      <header className=\"h-16 border-b border-white/10 flex items-center px-4 shrink-0\">\n        <button \n          onClick={() => setIsLibraryOpen(true)}\n          className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"\n        >\n          ☰ Library\n        </button>\n        <div className=\"flex-1\" />\n        {/* User menu */}\n      </header>\n\n      {/* Main Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 flex overflow-hidden\">\n        <ChatContainer \n          className=\"flex-1 min-w-[400px]\"\n          onTaskCreated={handleTaskCreated}\n          onOpenPanel={(taskId) => {\n            setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n            setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          }}\n        />\n\n        {/* Desktop Panel */}\n        {!isMobile && (\n          <div \n            className={cn(\n              \"transition-all duration-300 border-l border-white/10 bg-background\",\n              isPanelOpen ? \"w-[40%]\" : \"w-0 opacity-0 overflow-hidden\"\n            )}\n          >\n            {currentTaskId && (\n              <VideoDetailPanel \n                taskId={currentTaskId}\n                onClose={() => setIsPanelOpen(false)}\n              />\n            )}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Library Sidebar */}\n      <LibrarySidebar \n        isOpen={isLibraryOpen}\n        onClose={() => setIsLibraryOpen(false)}\n        onSelectTask={(taskId) => {\n          setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n          setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          setIsLibraryOpen(false)\n        }}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n### Validation\n- [ ] Renders split-screen on desktop (≥1024px)\n- [ ] Panel animates smoothly (300ms)\n- [ ] URL param `?task=xxx` auto-opens panel\n- [ ] Library sidebar opens with `Cmd+K`\n\n---\n\n## 2. ChatContainer\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatContainer.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nContains chat messages and input. Integrates with AI SDK **v6** for message handling.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatContainerProps {\n  className?: string\n  onTaskCreated?: (taskId: string) => void\n  onOpenPanel?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template (AI SDK v6)\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'  // ⚠️ MUST use @ai-sdk/react (v6)\nimport { useState } from 'react'\nimport { ChatMessages } from './ChatMessages'\nimport { ChatInput } from './ChatInput'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\n\nexport function ChatContainer({ \n  className, \n  onTaskCreated,\n  onOpenPanel \n}: ChatContainerProps) {\n  // AI SDK v6 useChat hook\n  const { messages, append, isLoading, error } = useChat({\n    api: '/api/chat',\n    onFinish: (message) => {\n      // Check if message contains task ID\n      const taskId = extractTaskId(message)\n      if (taskId && onTaskCreated) {\n        onTaskCreated(taskId)\n      }\n    }\n  })\n\n  const handleSubmit = async (content: string) => {\n    // v6 API: use append() to add user message\n    await append({ role: 'user', content })\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"flex flex-col h-full\", className)}>\n      <ChatMessages \n        messages={messages}\n        onOpenPanel={onOpenPanel}\n      />\n      <ChatInput \n        onSubmit={handleSubmit}\n        disabled={isLoading}\n        error={error?.message}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n\nfunction extractTaskId(message: any): string | null {\n  // Parse message for task_id in tool call result\n  // Implementation depends on AI SDK message format\n  return null\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. VideoCardMessage\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/messages/VideoCardMessage.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nDisplays video processing status as a card in chat messages.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoCardMessageProps {\n  taskId: string\n  videoUrl: string\n  title?: string\n  thumbnailUrl?: string\n  status: 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed'\n  progress?: number\n  onViewClick?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { Card } from '@/components/ui/card'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress'\nimport { Loader2, CheckCircle, XCircle } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function VideoCardMessage({\n  taskId,\n  videoUrl,\n  title,\n  thumbnailUrl,\n  status,\n  progress = 0,\n  onViewClick\n}: VideoCardMessageProps) {\n  const StatusIcon = {\n    processing: Loader2,\n    completed: CheckCircle,\n    failed: XCircle\n  }[status]\n\n  return (\n    <Card className=\"p-4 max-w-sm\">\n      {/* Thumbnail */}\n      {thumbnailUrl && (\n        <img \n          src={thumbnailUrl} \n          alt={title}\n          className=\"w-full aspect-video object-cover rounded-lg mb-3\"\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Title */}\n      <h3 className=\"font-semibold mb-2 line-clamp-2\">\n        {title || 'Processing video...'}\n      </h3>\n\n      {/* Status */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-3\">\n        <StatusIcon className={cn(\n          \"w-4 h-4\",\n          status === 'processing' && \"animate-spin\",\n          status === 'completed' && \"text-green-500\",\n          status === 'failed' && \"text-red-500\"\n        )} />\n        <span>\n          {status === 'processing' && `Processing (${progress}%)`}\n          {status === 'completed' && 'Ready'}\n          {status === 'failed' && 'Failed'}\n        </span>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Progress Bar */}\n      {status === 'processing' && (\n        <Progress value={progress} className=\"mb-3\" />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Action Button */}\n      {status === 'completed' && onViewClick && (\n        <Button \n          onClick={() => onViewClick(taskId)}\n          className=\"w-full\"\n        >\n          View Summary\n        </Button>\n      )}\n    </Card>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. VideoDetailPanel\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/VideoDetailPanel.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRight-side panel displaying video player and analysis results. Reuses extracted components from TaskDetailClient.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoDetailPanelProps {\n  taskId: string\n  onClose?: () => void\n  className?: string\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { VideoPlayer } from '@/components/tasks/VideoPlayer'\nimport { SummarySection } from '@/components/tasks/SummarySection'\nimport { ScriptTimeline } from '@/components/tasks/ScriptTimeline'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { X } from 'lucide-react'\nimport { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs'\n\nexport function VideoDetailPanel({ \n  taskId, \n  onClose, \n  className \n}: VideoDetailPanelProps) {\n  const [task, setTask] = useState(null)\n  const [outputs, setOutputs] = useState([])\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    // Fetch task and outputs\n    const fetchData = async () => {\n      const { data: taskData } = await supabase\n        .from('tasks')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('id', taskId)\n        .single()\n      \n      const { data: outputsData } = await supabase\n        .from('task_outputs')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('task_id', taskId)\n\n      setTask(taskData)\n      setOutputs(outputsData || [])\n    }\n\n    fetchData()\n\n    // Realtime subscription\n    const channel = supabase.channel(`task_${taskId}`)\n      .on('postgres_changes', {\n        event: '*',\n        schema: 'public',\n        table: 'tasks',\n        filter: `id=eq.${taskId}`\n      }, (payload) => {\n        setTask(payload.new)\n      })\n      .subscribe()\n\n    return () => {\n      supabase.removeChannel(channel)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  if (!task) return <div className=\"p-4\">Loading...</div>\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"h-full flex flex-col\", className)}>\n      {/* Header */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-b border-white/10\">\n        <h2 className=\"font-semibold truncate\">{task.video_title}</h2>\n        {onClose && (\n          <Button variant=\"ghost\" size=\"icon\" onClick={onClose}>\n            <X className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n          </Button>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4\">\n        <VideoPlayer \n          videoUrl={task.video_url}\n          title={task.video_title}\n          coverUrl={task.thumbnail_url}\n        />\n\n        <Tabs defaultValue=\"summary\">\n          <TabsList>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"summary\">Summary</TabsTrigger>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"script\">Script</TabsTrigger>\n          </TabsList>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"summary\">\n            <SummarySection \n              outputs={outputs}\n              taskTitle={task.video_title}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"script\">\n            <ScriptTimeline \n              scriptRawContent={outputs.find(o => o.kind === 'script_raw')?.content}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n        </Tabs>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. LibrarySidebar\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/LibrarySidebar.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nSlide-out sidebar showing user's task history.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface LibrarySidebarProps {\n  isOpen: boolean\n  onClose: () => void\n  onSelectTask: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { Sheet, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetTitle } from '@/components/ui/sheet'\nimport { Input } from '@/components/ui/input'\nimport { Search } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function LibrarySidebar({ \n  isOpen, \n  onClose, \n  onSelectTask \n}: LibrarySidebarProps) {\n  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState([])\n  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState('')\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (isOpen) {\n      fetchTasks()\n    }\n  }, [isOpen])\n\n  const fetchTasks = async () => {\n    const { data } = await supabase\n      .from('tasks')\n      .select('*')\n      .eq('status', 'completed')\n      .order('created_at', { ascending: false })\n      .limit(20)\n\n    setTasks(data || [])\n  }\n\n  const filteredTasks = tasks.filter(task =>\n    task.video_title?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())\n  )\n\n  return (\n    <Sheet open={isOpen} onOpenChange={onClose}>\n      <SheetContent side=\"left\" className=\"w-[320px] p-0\">\n        <SheetHeader className=\"p-4 border-b\">\n          <SheetTitle>Library</SheetTitle>\n        </SheetHeader>\n\n        {/* Search */}\n        <div className=\"p-4\">\n          <div className=\"relative\">\n            <Search className=\"absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-muted-foreground\" />\n            <Input\n              placeholder=\"Search videos...\"\n              value={searchQuery}\n              onChange={(e) => setSearchQuery(e.target.value)}\n              className=\"pl-9\"\n            />\n          </div>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* Task List */}\n        <div className=\"overflow-y-auto\">\n          {filteredTasks.map(task => (\n            <button\n              key={task.id}\n              onClick={() => {\n                onSelectTask(task.id)\n                onClose()\n              }}\n              className=\"w-full text-left p-4 hover:bg-white/5 transition-colors border-b border-white/5\"\n            >\n              <div className=\"font-medium truncate\">{task.video_title}</div>\n              <div className=\"text-sm text-muted-foreground\">\n                {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n              </div>\n            </button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      </SheetContent>\n    </Sheet>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Component Checklist\n\n**Before marking any component as complete**:\n\n- [ ] TypeScript types defined and exported\n- [ ] Props documented with JSDoc comments\n- [ ] Responsive behavior implemented (mobile/desktop)\n- [ ] Loading states handled\n- [ ] Error states handled\n- [ ] Accessibility attributes added (aria-*, role)\n- [ ] Unit tests written (if applicable)\n- [ ] Integrated with parent component\n\n**Next**: See `tasks/phase-2-frontend/task-2.1-chat-workspace.md` to start implementation.\n",
                "after": "# Component Specifications\n\n> **Purpose**: Technical specs for all new/refactored components  \n> **Audience**: Coding Agents, Frontend Developers  \n> **Format**: TypeScript interfaces + Implementation examples  \n> **Last Updated**: 2025-01-18\n\n---\n\n## ⚠️ Critical Dependency: AI SDK v6\n\n**IMPORTANT**: This project uses **AI SDK v6** (Vercel AI SDK). All implementations MUST use v6 APIs.\n\n```bash\n# Required packages (v6 ONLY)\nnpm install ai@^4.0.0 @ai-sdk/react@^1.0.0 @ai-sdk/openai@^1.0.0\n```\n\n**v6 Import Pattern**:\n```typescript\n// ✅ CORRECT - AI SDK v6\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'\n\n// ❌ WRONG - Old v3 pattern (DO NOT USE)\nimport { useChat } from 'ai/react'\n```\n\n**Key v6 Changes**:\n- `useChat` returns `{ messages, sendMessage, status }` (not `handleSubmit`)\n- Use `sendMessage({ content: string })` instead of form submission\n- `status` values: `'idle' | 'streaming' | 'error'`\n\n---\n\n## 📦 Component Hierarchy\n\n```\nChatWorkspace (New - Root Container)\n├── ChatContainer (New)\n│   ├── ChatMessages (Refactor AssistantChat)\n│   ├── MessageItem (New)\n│   │   ├── VideoCardMessage (New)\n│   │   ├── TextMessage (Exists)\n│   │   └── ProcessingMessage (New)\n│   └── ChatInput (New)\n│\n├── VideoDetailPanel (New)\n│   ├── VideoPlayer (Extract from TaskDetailClient)\n│   ├── SummarySection (Extract from TaskDetailClient)\n│   ├── ScriptTimeline (Reuse existing)\n│   └── MindMapView (Extract from TaskDetailClient)\n│\n└── LibrarySidebar (New)\n    ├── LibrarySearch (New)\n    └── TaskListItem (New)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 1. ChatWorkspace\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatWorkspace.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRoot container for the split-screen chat interface. Manages layout and state coordination between chat and video panel.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatWorkspaceProps {\n  // No props - uses URL params and context\n}\n```\n\n### Local State\n```typescript\nconst [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(false)\nconst [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(null)\nconst [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\nconst [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useState, useEffect } from 'react'\nimport { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\nimport { ChatContainer } from './ChatContainer'\nimport { VideoDetailPanel } from './VideoDetailPanel'\nimport { LibrarySidebar } from './LibrarySidebar'\n\nexport function ChatWorkspace() {\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams()\n  const taskId = searchParams.get('task')\n  \n  const [isPanelOpen, setIsPanelOpen] = useState(!!taskId)\n  const [currentTaskId, setCurrentTaskId] = useState<string | null>(taskId)\n  const [isLibraryOpen, setIsLibraryOpen] = useState(false)\n  const [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(false)\n\n  // Detect mobile\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const checkMobile = () => setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 768)\n    checkMobile()\n    window.addEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', checkMobile)\n  }, [])\n\n  // Open panel when task ID changes\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (taskId) {\n      setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n      setIsPanelOpen(true)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  const handleTaskCreated = (newTaskId: string) => {\n    setCurrentTaskId(newTaskId)\n    setIsPanelOpen(true)\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"h-screen flex flex-col\">\n      {/* Top Bar */}\n      <header className=\"h-16 border-b border-white/10 flex items-center px-4 shrink-0\">\n        <button \n          onClick={() => setIsLibraryOpen(true)}\n          className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"\n        >\n          ☰ Library\n        </button>\n        <div className=\"flex-1\" />\n        {/* User menu */}\n      </header>\n\n      {/* Main Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 flex overflow-hidden\">\n        <ChatContainer \n          className=\"flex-1 min-w-[400px]\"\n          onTaskCreated={handleTaskCreated}\n          onOpenPanel={(taskId) => {\n            setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n            setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          }}\n        />\n\n        {/* Desktop Panel */}\n        {!isMobile && (\n          <div \n            className={cn(\n              \"transition-all duration-300 border-l border-white/10 bg-background\",\n              isPanelOpen ? \"w-[40%]\" : \"w-0 opacity-0 overflow-hidden\"\n            )}\n          >\n            {currentTaskId && (\n              <VideoDetailPanel \n                taskId={currentTaskId}\n                onClose={() => setIsPanelOpen(false)}\n              />\n            )}\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Library Sidebar */}\n      <LibrarySidebar \n        isOpen={isLibraryOpen}\n        onClose={() => setIsLibraryOpen(false)}\n        onSelectTask={(taskId) => {\n          setCurrentTaskId(taskId)\n          setIsPanelOpen(true)\n          setIsLibraryOpen(false)\n        }}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n### Validation\n- [ ] Renders split-screen on desktop (≥1024px)\n- [ ] Panel animates smoothly (300ms)\n- [ ] URL param `?task=xxx` auto-opens panel\n\n---\n\n## 2. ChatContainer\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/ChatContainer.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nContains chat messages and input. Integrates with AI SDK **v6** for message handling.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface ChatContainerProps {\n  className?: string\n  onTaskCreated?: (taskId: string) => void\n  onOpenPanel?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template (AI SDK v6)\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useChat } from '@ai-sdk/react'  // ⚠️ MUST use @ai-sdk/react (v6)\nimport { useState } from 'react'\nimport { ChatMessages } from './ChatMessages'\nimport { ChatInput } from './ChatInput'\nimport { cn } from '@/lib/utils'\n\nexport function ChatContainer({ \n  className, \n  onTaskCreated,\n  onOpenPanel \n}: ChatContainerProps) {\n  // AI SDK v6 useChat hook\n  const { messages, append, isLoading, error } = useChat({\n    api: '/api/chat',\n    onFinish: (message) => {\n      // Check if message contains task ID\n      const taskId = extractTaskId(message)\n      if (taskId && onTaskCreated) {\n        onTaskCreated(taskId)\n      }\n    }\n  })\n\n  const handleSubmit = async (content: string) => {\n    // v6 API: use append() to add user message\n    await append({ role: 'user', content })\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"flex flex-col h-full\", className)}>\n      <ChatMessages \n        messages={messages}\n        onOpenPanel={onOpenPanel}\n      />\n      <ChatInput \n        onSubmit={handleSubmit}\n        disabled={isLoading}\n        error={error?.message}\n      />\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n\nfunction extractTaskId(message: any): string | null {\n  // Parse message for task_id in tool call result\n  // Implementation depends on AI SDK message format\n  return null\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. VideoCardMessage\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/messages/VideoCardMessage.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nDisplays video processing status as a card in chat messages.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoCardMessageProps {\n  taskId: string\n  videoUrl: string\n  title?: string\n  thumbnailUrl?: string\n  status: 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed'\n  progress?: number\n  onViewClick?: (taskId: string) => void\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { Card } from '@/components/ui/card'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress'\nimport { Loader2, CheckCircle, XCircle } from 'lucide-react'\n\nexport function VideoCardMessage({\n  taskId,\n  videoUrl,\n  title,\n  thumbnailUrl,\n  status,\n  progress = 0,\n  onViewClick\n}: VideoCardMessageProps) {\n  const StatusIcon = {\n    processing: Loader2,\n    completed: CheckCircle,\n    failed: XCircle\n  }[status]\n\n  return (\n    <Card className=\"p-4 max-w-sm\">\n      {/* Thumbnail */}\n      {thumbnailUrl && (\n        <img \n          src={thumbnailUrl} \n          alt={title}\n          className=\"w-full aspect-video object-cover rounded-lg mb-3\"\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Title */}\n      <h3 className=\"font-semibold mb-2 line-clamp-2\">\n        {title || 'Processing video...'}\n      </h3>\n\n      {/* Status */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-muted-foreground mb-3\">\n        <StatusIcon className={cn(\n          \"w-4 h-4\",\n          status === 'processing' && \"animate-spin\",\n          status === 'completed' && \"text-green-500\",\n          status === 'failed' && \"text-red-500\"\n        )} />\n        <span>\n          {status === 'processing' && `Processing (${progress}%)`}\n          {status === 'completed' && 'Ready'}\n          {status === 'failed' && 'Failed'}\n        </span>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Progress Bar */}\n      {status === 'processing' && (\n        <Progress value={progress} className=\"mb-3\" />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Action Button */}\n      {status === 'completed' && onViewClick && (\n        <Button \n          onClick={() => onViewClick(taskId)}\n          className=\"w-full\"\n        >\n          View Summary\n        </Button>\n      )}\n    </Card>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. VideoDetailPanel\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/VideoDetailPanel.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nRight-side panel displaying video player and analysis results. Reuses extracted components from TaskDetailClient.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface VideoDetailPanelProps {\n  taskId: string\n  onClose?: () => void\n  className?: string\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation Template\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from 'react'\nimport { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase'\nimport { VideoPlayer } from '@/components/tasks/VideoPlayer'\nimport { SummarySection } from '@/components/tasks/SummarySection'\nimport { ScriptTimeline } from '@/components/tasks/ScriptTimeline'\nimport { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'\nimport { X } from 'lucide-react'\nimport { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs'\n\nexport function VideoDetailPanel({ \n  taskId, \n  onClose, \n  className \n}: VideoDetailPanelProps) {\n  const [task, setTask] = useState(null)\n  const [outputs, setOutputs] = useState([])\n  const supabase = createClient()\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    // Fetch task and outputs\n    const fetchData = async () => {\n      const { data: taskData } = await supabase\n        .from('tasks')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('id', taskId)\n        .single()\n      \n      const { data: outputsData } = await supabase\n        .from('task_outputs')\n        .select('*')\n        .eq('task_id', taskId)\n\n      setTask(taskData)\n      setOutputs(outputsData || [])\n    }\n\n    fetchData()\n\n    // Realtime subscription\n    const channel = supabase.channel(`task_${taskId}`)\n      .on('postgres_changes', {\n        event: '*',\n        schema: 'public',\n        table: 'tasks',\n        filter: `id=eq.${taskId}`\n      }, (payload) => {\n        setTask(payload.new)\n      })\n      .subscribe()\n\n    return () => {\n      supabase.removeChannel(channel)\n    }\n  }, [taskId])\n\n  if (!task) return <div className=\"p-4\">Loading...</div>\n\n  return (\n    <div className={cn(\"h-full flex flex-col\", className)}>\n      {/* Header */}\n      <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between p-4 border-b border-white/10\">\n        <h2 className=\"font-semibold truncate\">{task.video_title}</h2>\n        {onClose && (\n          <Button variant=\"ghost\" size=\"icon\" onClick={onClose}>\n            <X className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n          </Button>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Content */}\n      <div className=\"flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 space-y-4\">\n        <VideoPlayer \n          videoUrl={task.video_url}\n          title={task.video_title}\n          coverUrl={task.thumbnail_url}\n        />\n\n        <Tabs defaultValue=\"summary\">\n          <TabsList>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"summary\">Summary</TabsTrigger>\n            <TabsTrigger value=\"script\">Script</TabsTrigger>\n          </TabsList>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"summary\">\n            <SummarySection \n              outputs={outputs}\n              taskTitle={task.video_title}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n\n          <TabsContent value=\"script\">\n            <ScriptTimeline \n              scriptRawContent={outputs.find(o => o.kind === 'script_raw')?.content}\n            />\n          </TabsContent>\n        </Tabs>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. LibrarySidebar\n\n**File Path**: `frontend/src/components/chat/LibrarySidebar.tsx`\n\n### Purpose\nSlide-out sidebar showing user's task history.\n\n### Props Interface\n```typescript\ninterface .limit(20)\n\n    setTasks(data || [])\n  }\n\n  const filteredTasks = tasks.filter(task =>\n    task.video_title?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())\n  )\n\n  return (\n    <Sheet     </SheetHeader>\n\n        {/* Search */}\n        <div className=\"p-4\">\n          <div className=\"relative\">\n            <Search className=\"absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-muted-foreground\">\n                {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n              </div>\n            </button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      </SheetContent>\n    </Sheet>\n  )\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ Component Checklist\n\n**Before marking any component as complete**:\n\n- [ ] TypeScript types defined and exported\n- [ ] Props documented with JSDoc comments\n- complete without errors\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 4: Create Utility Functions\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/lib/url-utils.ts`\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Utility functions for URL detection and validation\n */\n\nconst YOUTUBE_REGEX = /(?:https?:\\/\\/)?(?:www\\.)?(?:youtube\\.com\\/watch\\?v=|youtu\\.be\\/)([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})/g\nconst BILIBILI_REGEX = /(?:https?:\\/\\/)?(?:www\\.)?bilibili\\.com\\/video\\/(BV[a-zA-Z0-9]+)/g\n\n/**\n * Detect all YouTube URLs in a string\n */\nexport function detectYouTubeURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  const matches = text.matchAll(YOUTUBE_REGEX)\n  return Array.from(matches, m => m[0])\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect all Bilibili URLs in a string\n */\nexport function detectBilibiliURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  const matches = text.matchAll(BILIBILI_REGEX)\n  return Array.from(matches, m => m[0])\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect all video URLs (YouTube + Bilibili)\n */\nexport function detectVideoURLs(text: string): string[] {\n  return [\n    ...detectYouTubeURLs(text),\n    ...detectBilibiliURLs(text)\n  ]\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if text contains multiple video URLs\n */\nexport function hasMultipleURLs(text: string): boolean {\n  return detectVideoURLs(text).length > 1\n}\n\n/**\n * Extract video ID from YouTube URL\n */\nexport function extractYouTubeID(url: string): string | null {\n  const match = url.match(YOUTUBE_REGEX)\n  return match ? match[1] : null\n}\n```\n\n**Verification**:\n```bash\n# Create test file\ncat > frontend/src/lib/__tests__/url-utils.test.ts << 'EOF'\nimport { detectVideoURLs, hasMultipleURLs } from '../url-utils'\n\ntest('detects single YouTube URL', () => {\n  const text = \"Check this out: https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123\"\n  expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(1)\n  expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(false)\n})\n\ntest('detects multiple URLs', () => {\n  const text = \"https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123 and https://youtube.com/watch?v=def456\"\n  expect(detectVideoURLs(text)).toHaveLength(2)\n  expect(hasMultipleURLs(text)).toBe(true)\n})\nEOF\n\n# Run tests\nnpm test -- url-utils.test.ts\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 5: Update Supabase Types (if needed)\n\n**File**: `frontend/src/types/database.ts`\n\nAdd or verify chat-related tables:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface You can call multiple tools in a single response. Use a HEREDOC to pass the body to ensure correct formatting.\\\\n<example>\\\\ngh pr create --title \\\\\\\"the pr title\\\\\\\" --body \\\\\\\"$(cat <<'EOF'\\\\n## Summary\\\\n<1-3 bullet points>\\\\n</example>\\\\n\\\\nImportant:\\\\n- DO NOT use the TodoWrite or Task tools\\\\n- Return the PR URL when you're done, so the user can see it\\\\n\\\\n# Other common operations\\\\n- View comments on a Github PR: gh api repos/foo/bar/pulls/123/comments\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"command\\\",\\\"description\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"command\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The command to execute\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"timeout\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"Optional timeout in milliseconds\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"number\\\"},\\\"workdir\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The working directory to run the command in. Use this instead of 'cd' commands.\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"description\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"Clear, concise description of what this command does in 5-10 words. You can access any file directly by using this tool.\\\\nAssume this tool is able to read all files on the machine. If the User provides a path to a file assume that path is valid. It is okay to read a file that does not exist; an error will be returned.\\\\n\\\\nUsage:\\\\n- The filePath parameter must be an absolute path, not a relative path\\\\n- By default, it reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file\\\\n- You can optionally specify a line offset and limit (especially handy for long files), but it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters\\\\n- Any lines longer than 2000 characters will be truncated\\\\n- Results are returned using cat -n format, with line numbers starting at 1\\\\n- You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. It is always better to speculatively read multiple files as a batch that are potentially useful.\\\\n- If you read a file that exists but has empty contents you will receive a system reminder warning in place of file contents.\\\\n- You can read image files using this tool.\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"filePath\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"filePath\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The path to the file to read\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"offset\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The line number to start reading from (0-based)\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"number\\\"},\\\"limit\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The number of lines to read (defaults to 2000)\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"number\\\"}}}},{\\\"name\\\":\\\"glob\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"- Fast file pattern matching tool that works with any codebase size\\\\n- Supports glob patterns like \\\\\\\"**/*.js\\\\\\\" or \\\\\\\"src/**/*.ts\\\\\\\"\\\\n- Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time\\\\n- Use this tool when you need to find files by name patterns\\\\n- When you are doing an open-ended search that may require multiple rounds of globbing and grepping, use the Task tool instead\\\\n- You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. It is always better to speculatively perform multiple searches as a batch that are potentially useful.\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"pattern\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"pattern\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The glob pattern to match files against\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"path\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The directory to search in. DO NOT enter \\\\\\\"undefined\\\\\\\" or \\\\\\\"null\\\\\\\" - simply omit it for the default behavior. Must be a valid directory path if provided.\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"}}}},{\\\"name\\\":\\\"grep\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"- Fast content search tool that works with any codebase size\\\\n- Searches file contents using regular expressions\\\\n- Supports full regex syntax (eg. \\\\\\\"log.*Error\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"function\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\", etc.)\\\\n- Filter files by pattern with the include parameter (eg. \\\\\\\"*.js\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"*.{ts,tsx}\\\\\\\")\\\\n- Returns file paths and line numbers with at least one match sorted by modification time\\\\n- Use this tool when you need to find files containing specific patterns\\\\n- If you need to identify/count the number of matches within files, use the Bash tool with `rg` (ripgrep) directly. Do NOT use `grep`.\\\\n- When you are doing an open-ended search that may require multiple rounds of globbing and grepping, use the Task tool instead\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"pattern\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"pattern\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The regex pattern to search for in file contents\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"path\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The directory to search in. This tool will error if you attempt an edit without reading the file. \\\\n- When editing text from Read tool output, ensure you preserve the exact indentation (tabs/spaces) as it appears AFTER the line number prefix. The line number prefix format is: spaces + line number + tab. Everything after that tab is the actual file content to match. Never include any part of the line number prefix in the oldString or newString.\\\\n- ALWAYS prefer editing existing files in the codebase. Use this agent to execute multiple units of work in parallel.\\\\n- explore: Fast agent specialized for exploring codebases. Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible, to maximize performance; to do that, use a single message with multiple tool uses\\\\n2. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.\\\\n3. Each agent invocation is stateless unless you provide a session_id. Your prompt should contain a highly detailed task description for the agent to perform autonomously and you should specify exactly what information the agent should return back to you in its final and only message to you.\\\\n4. The agent's outputs should generally be trusted\\\\n5. Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent\\\\n6. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.\\\\nIt also helps the user understand the progress of the task and overall progress of their requests.\\\\n\\\\n## When to Use This Tool\\\\nUse this tool proactively in these scenarios:\\\\n\\\\n1. Complex multistep tasks - When a task requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions\\\\n2. Non-trivial and complex tasks - Tasks that require careful planning or multiple operations\\\\n3. User explicitly requests todo list - When the user directly asks you to use the todo list\\\\n4. User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)\\\\n5. After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as todos. Feel free to edit the todo list based on new information.\\\\n6. After completing a task - Mark it complete and add any new follow-up tasks\\\\n7. When you start working on a new task, mark the todo as in_progress. Ideally you should only have one todo as in_progress at a time. Complete existing tasks before starting new ones.\\\\n\\\\n## When NOT to Use This Tool\\\\n\\\\nSkip using this tool when:\\\\n1. There is only a single, straightforward task\\\\n2. The task is purely conversational or informational\\\\n\\\\nNOTE that you should not use this tool if there is only one trivial task to do. AVOID using `cd <directory> && <command>` patterns - use `workdir` instead.\\\\n\\\\nIMPORTANT: This tool is for terminal operations like git, npm, docker, etc. DO NOT use it for file operations (reading, writing, editing, searching, finding files) - use the specialized tools for this instead.\\\\n\\\\nBefore executing the command, please follow these steps:\\\\n\\\\n1. Directory Verification:\\\\n   - If the command will create new directories or files, first use `ls` to verify the parent directory exists and is the correct location\\\\n   - For example, before running \\\\\\\"mkdir foo/bar\\\\\\\", first use `ls foo` to check that \\\\\\\"foo\\\\\\\" exists and is the intended parent directory\\\\n\\\\n2. Command Execution:\\\\n   - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes (e.g., rm \\\\\\\"path with spaces/file.txt\\\\\\\")\\\\n   - Examples of proper quoting:\\\\n     - mkdir \\\\\\\"/Users/name/My Documents\\\\\\\" (correct)\\\\n     - mkdir /Users/name/My Documents (incorrect - will fail)\\\\n     - python \\\\\\\"/path/with spaces/script.py\\\\\\\" (correct)\\\\n     - python /path/with spaces/script.py (incorrect - will fail)\\\\n   - After ensuring proper quoting, execute the command.\\\\n   - Capture the output of the command.\\\\n\\\\nUsage notes:\\\\n  - The command argument is required.\\\\n  - You can specify an optional timeout in milliseconds. If not specified, commands will time out after 120000ms (2 minutes).\\\\n  - It is very helpful if you write a clear, concise description of what this command does in 5-10 words.\\\\n  - If the output exceeds 2000 lines or 51200 bytes, it will be truncated and the full output will be written to a file. You can use Read with offset/limit to read specific sections or Grep to search the full content. Because of this, you do NOT need to use `head`, `tail`, or other truncation commands to limit output - just run the command directly.\\\\n\\\\n  - Avoid using Bash with the `find`, `grep`, `cat`, `head`, `tail`, `sed`, `awk`, or `echo` commands, unless explicitly instructed or when these commands are truly necessary for the task. Instead, always prefer using the dedicated tools for these commands:\\\\n    - File search: Use Glob (NOT find or ls)\\\\n    - Content search: Use Grep (NOT grep or rg)\\\\n    - Read files: Use Read (NOT cat/head/tail)\\\\n    - Edit files: Use Edit (NOT sed/awk)\\\\n    - Write files: Use Write (NOT echo >/cat <<EOF)\\\\n    - Communication: Output text directly (NOT echo/printf)\\\\n  - When issuing multiple commands:\\\\n    - If the commands are independent and can run in parallel, make multiple Bash tool calls in a single message. For example, if you need to run \\\\\\\"git status\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"git diff\\\\\\\", send a single message with two Bash tool calls in parallel.\\\\n    - If the commands depend on each other and must run sequentially, use a single Bash call with '&&' to chain them together (e.g., `git add . && git commit -m \\\\\\\"message\\\\\\\" && git push`). For instance, if one operation must complete before another starts (like mkdir before cp, Write before Bash for git operations, or git add before git commit), run these operations sequentially instead.\\\\n    - Use ';' only when you need to run commands sequentially but don't care if earlier commands fail\\\\n    - DO NOT use newlines to separate commands (newlines are ok in quoted strings)\\\\n  - AVOID using `cd <directory> && <command>`. Use the `workdir` parameter to change directories instead.\\\\n    <good-example>\\\\n    Use workdir=\\\\\\\"/foo/bar\\\\\\\" with command: pytest tests\\\\n    </good-example>\\\\n    <bad-example>\\\\n    cd /foo/bar && pytest tests\\\\n    </bad-example>\\\\n\\\\n# Committing changes with git\\\\n\\\\nOnly create commits when requested by the user. If unclear, ask first. When the user asks you to create a new git commit, follow these steps carefully:\\\\n\\\\nGit Safety Protocol:\\\\n- NEVER update the git config\\\\n- NEVER run destructive/irreversible git commands (like push --force, hard reset, etc) unless the user explicitly requests them\\\\n- NEVER skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign, etc) unless the user explicitly requests it\\\\n- NEVER run force push to main/master, warn the user if they request it\\\\n- Avoid git commit --amend. ONLY use --amend when ALL conditions are met:\\\\n  (1) User explicitly requested amend, OR commit SUCCEEDED but pre-commit hook auto-modified files that need including\\\\n  (2) HEAD commit was created by you in this conversation (verify: git log -1 --format='%an %ae')\\\\n  (3) Commit has NOT been pushed to remote (verify: git status shows \\\\\\\"Your branch is ahead\\\\\\\")\\\\n- CRITICAL: If commit FAILED or was REJECTED by hook, NEVER amend - fix the issue and create a NEW commit\\\\n- CRITICAL: If you already pushed to remote, NEVER amend unless user explicitly requests it (requires force push)\\\\n- NEVER commit changes unless the user explicitly asks you to. It is VERY IMPORTANT to only commit when explicitly asked, otherwise the user will feel that you are being too proactive.\\\\n\\\\n1. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following bash commands in parallel, each using the Bash tool:\\\\n  - Run a git status command to see all untracked files.\\\\n  - Run a git diff command to see both staged and unstaged changes that will be committed.\\\\n  - Run a git log command to see recent commit messages, so that you can follow this repository's commit message style.\\\\n2. Analyze all staged changes (both previously staged and newly added) and draft a commit message:\\\\n  - Summarize the nature of the changes (eg. new feature, enhancement to an existing feature, bug fix, refactoring, test, docs, etc.). Ensure the message accurately reflects the changes and their purpose (i.e. \\\\\\\"add\\\\\\\" means a wholly new feature, \\\\\\\"update\\\\\\\" means an enhancement to an existing feature, \\\\\\\"fix\\\\\\\" means a bug fix, etc.).\\\\n  - Do not commit files that likely contain secrets (.env, credentials.json, etc.). Warn the user if they specifically request to commit those files\\\\n  - Draft a concise (1-2 sentences) commit message that focuses on the \\\\\\\"why\\\\\\\" rather than the \\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\"\\\\n  - Ensure it accurately reflects the changes and their purpose\\\\n3. You can call multiple tools in a single response. When multiple independent pieces of information are requested and all commands are likely to succeed, run multiple tool calls in parallel for optimal performance. run the following commands:\\\\n   - Add relevant untracked files to the staging area.\\\\n   - Create the commit with a message\\\\n   - Run git status after the commit completes to verify success.\\\\n   Note: git status depends on the commit completing, so run it sequentially after the commit.\\\\n4. If the commit fails due to pre-commit hook, fix the issue and create a NEW commit (see amend rules above)\\\\n\\\\nImportant notes:\\\\n- NEVER run additional commands to read or explore code, besides git bash commands\\\\n- NEVER use the TodoWrite or Task tools\\\\n- DO NOT push to the remote repository unless the user explicitly asks you to do so\\\\n- IMPORTANT: Never use git commands with the -i flag (like git rebase -i or git add -i) since they require interactive input which is not supported.\\\\n- If there are no changes to commit (i.e., no untracked files and no modifications), do not create an empty commit\\\\n\\\\n# Creating pull requests\\\\nUse the gh command via the Bash tool for ALL GitHub-related tasks including working with issues, pull requests, checks, and releases. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.\\\\nIt also helps the user understand the progress of the task and overall progress of their requests.\\\\n\\\\n## When to Use This Tool\\\\nUse this tool proactively in these scenarios:\\\\n\\\\n1. When you start working on a new task, mark the todo as in_progress. Ideally you should only have one todo as in_progress at a time. For example: Task(description=\\\\\\\"Check the file\\\\\\\", prompt=\\\\\\\"/check-file path/to/file.py\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\nWhen NOT to use the Task tool:\\\\n- If you want to read a specific file path, use the Read or Glob tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly\\\\n- If you are searching for a specific class definition like \\\\\\\"class Foo\\\\\\\", use the Glob tool instead, to find the match more quickly\\\\n- If you are searching for code within a specific file or set of 2-3 files, use the Read tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly\\\\n- Other tasks that are not related to the agent descriptions above\\\\n\\\\n\\\\nUsage notes:\\\\n1. Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible, to maximize performance; to do that, use a single message with multiple tool uses\\\\n2. When the agent is done, it will return a single message back to you. The result returned by the agent is not visible to the user. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.\\\\n3. Each agent invocation is stateless unless you provide a session_id. Your prompt should contain a highly detailed task description for the agent to perform autonomously and you should specify exactly what information the agent should return back to you in its final and only message to you.\\\\n4. The agent's outputs should generally be trusted\\\\n5. Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent\\\\n6. If the agent description mentions that it should be used proactively, then you should try your best to use it without the user having to ask for it first. Use your judgement.\\\\n\\\\nExample usage (NOTE: The agents below are fictional examples for illustration only - use the actual agents listed Use this instead of 'cd' commands.\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"description\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"Clear, concise description of what this command does in 5-10 words. You can access any file directly by using this tool.\\\\nAssume this tool is able to read all files on the machine. If the User provides a path to a file assume that path is valid. It is okay to read a file that does not exist; an error will be returned.\\\\n\\\\nUsage:\\\\n- The filePath parameter must be an absolute path, not a relative path\\\\n- By default, it reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file\\\\n- You can optionally specify a line offset and limit (especially handy for long files), but it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters\\\\n- Any lines longer than 2000 characters will be truncated\\\\n- Results are returned using cat -n format, with line numbers starting at 1\\\\n- You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. It is always better to speculatively read multiple files as a batch that are potentially useful.\\\\n- If you read a file that exists but has empty contents you will receive a system reminder warning in place of file contents.\\\\n- You can read image files using this tool.\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"filePath\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"filePath\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The path to the file to read\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"offset\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The line number to start reading from (0-based)\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"number\\\"},\\\"limit\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The number of lines to read (defaults to 2000)\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"number\\\"}}}},{\\\"name\\\":\\\"glob\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"- Fast file pattern matching tool that works with any codebase size\\\\n- Supports glob patterns like \\\\\\\"**/*.js\\\\\\\" or \\\\\\\"src/**/*.ts\\\\\\\"\\\\n- Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time\\\\n- Use this tool when you need to find files by name patterns\\\\n- When you are doing an open-ended search that may require multiple rounds of globbing and grepping, use the Task tool instead\\\\n- You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. It is always better to speculatively perform multiple searches as a batch that are potentially useful.\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"pattern\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"pattern\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The glob pattern to match files against\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"path\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The directory to search in. Must be a valid directory path if provided.\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"}}}},{\\\"name\\\":\\\"grep\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"- Fast content search tool that works with any codebase size\\\\n- Searches file contents using regular expressions\\\\n- Supports full regex syntax (eg. \\\\\\\"log.*Error\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"function\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\", etc.)\\\\n- Filter files by pattern with the include parameter (eg. \\\\\\\"*.js\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"*.{ts,tsx}\\\\\\\")\\\\n- Returns file paths and line numbers with at least one match sorted by modification time\\\\n- Use this tool when you need to find files containing specific patterns\\\\n- If you need to identify/count the number of matches within files, use the Bash tool with `rg` (ripgrep) directly. Do NOT use `grep`.\\\\n- When you are doing an open-ended search that may require multiple rounds of globbing and grepping, use the Task tool instead\\\\n\\\",\\\"parameters\\\":{\\\"required\\\":[\\\"pattern\\\"],\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"pattern\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The regex pattern to search for in file contents\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\"},\\\"path\\\":{\\\"description\\\":\\\"The directory to search in. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.\\\\n3. Each agent invocation is stateless unless you provide a session_id. Your prompt should contain a highly detailed task description for the agent to perform autonomously and you should specify exactly what information the agent should return back to you in its final and only message to you.\\\\n4. The agent's outputs should generally be trusted\\\\n5. Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent\\\\n6. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.\\\\nIt also helps the user understand the progress of the task and overall progress of their requests.\\\\n\\\\n## When to Use This Tool\\\\nUse this tool proactively in these scenarios:\\\\n\\\\n1. Complex multistep tasks - When a task requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions\\\\n2. Non-trivial and complex tasks - Tasks that require careful planning or multiple operations\\\\n3. User explicitly requests todo list - When the user directly asks you to use the todo list\\\\n4. Feel free to edit the todo list based on new information.\\\\n6. After completing a task - Mark it complete and add any new follow-up tasks\\\\n7. When you start working on a new task, mark the todo as in_progress. Ideally you should only have one todo as in_progress at a time. Complete existing tasks before starting new ones.\\\\n\\\\n## When NOT to Use This Tool\\\\n\\\\nSkip using this tool when:\\\\n1. There is only a single, straightforward task\\\\n2.
